target.title: fix(telegram): rethrow dispatch errors so spooled updates are not silently deleted
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Telegram spooled update message loss by propagating dispatch errors through the handler chain and debouncer, so `bot.handleUpdate()` rejects when the message processor fails and the spooled update consumer releases the claim for retry instead of deleting it.

## Root cause

When `dispatchTelegramMessage` throws inside `processMessage`, the error was caught and swallowed at two levels:

1. **`processMessage`** (`extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.ts`): The catch block sent a user-visible fallback but did not rethrow, so the caller saw success.
2. **`createInboundDebouncer`** (`src/auto-reply/inbound-debounce.ts`): Even when the handler does throw, `runFlush` catches all `onFlush` errors and routes them to `onError` without rethrowing, so `bot.handleUpdate()` always resolves — causing `#handleClaimedSpooledUpdate` to delete the claimed update instead of releasing it for retry.

## Fix

### 1. Rethrow in `processMessage`

After sending the user-visible fallback message, rethrow the dispatch error so callers can distinguish success from failure.

### 2. Add `rethrowOnFlushError` to the inbound debouncer

Add an opt-in `rethrowOnFlushError` option to `createInboundDebouncer`. When enabled, `runFlush` rethrows the error after calling `onError`, propagating the failure through `enqueue()` → middleware → `bot.handleUpdate()` → spooled update consumer.

Enabled this option in the Telegram handler debouncer.

**Other debouncer users are unaffected** — the option defaults to `false`.

### 3. Fix oxlint `only-throw-error` lint

Wrap the `unknown`-typed `flushError` with `instanceof Error` before rethrowing to satisfy the oxlint `typescript(only-throw-error)` rule.

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests for `processMessage` error rethrow

All 3 dispatch-failure tests now assert rejection instead of resolution:

- `"sends user-visible fallback and rethrows when dispatch throws"` — `processMessage` rejects after fallback
- `"omits message_thread_id for General-topic fallback replies"` — thread param omitted for General topic
- `"rethrows after sending fallback even when fallback delivery fails"` — rethrow wins even if fallback send also fails

### Unit tests for debouncer rethrow

- `"rethrows flush errors when rethrowOnFlushError is set"` — new test asserting error propagation

### Test output

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts
# 10 passed — 3 dispatch-failure tests now assert rejection

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts
# 62 passed — includes new rethrow test, existing tests unchanged
```

## CI status

- `check-lint` — ✅ Fixed (wrapped unknown flushError with instanceof Error)
- `checks-node-agentic-gateway-methods` — ❌ Pre-existing flaky test (sessionStartedAt timing), unrelated to this PR
- `Real behavior proof` — ❌ No real Telegram bot available for live testing; unit tests cover the deterministic error propagation paths completely

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Spooled Telegram updates are silently deleted on dispatch failure instead of being released for retry.
- Real environment tested: Linux (RHEL), Node.js 22.x, local checkout on branch `fix/issue-92129-telegram-spooled-message-loss`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts
```
- Evidence after fix: Both test suites pass. The `processMessage` tests prove that dispatch errors now propagate as rejections, and the debouncer tests prove existing behavior is unchanged.
- Observed result after fix: Both tests pass confirming dispatch errors propagate as rejections through handler and debouncer chain.
- What was not tested: Live Telegram dispatch with a real bot (no Telegram bot token available). This fix changes deterministic error propagation paths only — the `throw err` in `processMessage` and the `rethrowOnFlushError` branch in the debouncer. The unit tests directly assert the contract change: `processMessage` now rejects instead of resolving when dispatch fails, and the debouncer with `rethrowOnFlushError: true` propagates flush errors to the enqueue caller. These are async/await control-flow assertions, not integration-dependent behavior.

## Risk

Low. The `throw err` in `processMessage` is an additive change on an existing error path — the fallback is still sent. The `rethrowOnFlushError` option defaults to `false` and is only enabled for Telegram. Other debouncer users (Discord, WhatsApp, Feishu, MS Teams, Mattermost) are unaffected.

## Verification

- `pnpm test:changed` — all changed files pass
- Unit tests confirm:
  - Dispatch failure → processMessage rejects instead of resolving
  - Fallback message is still sent before rethrow
  - Fallback delivery failure does not prevent rethrow
  - Debouncer with `rethrowOnFlushError: false` (default) still swallows errors (backward compat)
  - Debouncer with `rethrowOnFlushError: true` propagates flush errors up to the caller
- `oxlint` — clean
- Import cycles — clean

target.title: fix(gateway): build row metadata context for single session lists
target.body:
## Summary

This fixes a residual `sessions.list` single-row performance path: when a safe list request is reduced to exactly one row (`limit=1`), the row builder skipped the shared session-list metadata context and fell back to uncached per-row subagent metadata lookups.

The change builds the request-scoped row metadata context whenever the selected row set is non-empty, for both sync and async session list builders:

```ts
entries.length > 0 ? buildSessionListRowMetadataContext({ now }) : undefined
```

It also adds a focused regression in `session-utils.single-row-cache.test.ts` covering `listSessionsFromStore()` and `listSessionsFromStoreAsync()` with `agentId` + `limit: 1`.

## Duplication / overlap gate

Checked before opening this PR:

- Current `origin/main` still has `entries.length > 1` in both sync and async session-list builders.
- Recent upstream commits did not touch `src/gateway/session-utils.ts` or `src/gateway/session-utils.single-row-cache.test.ts`.
- #90814 / #90819 are adjacent but not duplicates: they address plugin workspace-dir concurrency causing O(rows) metadata scans; this PR addresses the single-row shared rowContext gap. The #90819 branch still has the target `entries.length > 1` condition.
- #81108 is adjacent and touches session utilities, but its `entries.length > 0` logic is for checkpoint preview maps; it leaves this shared rowContext condition unchanged.
- Historical related work (#86788/#86794, #88166, #75013, #74970, #77187) improved nearby sessions.list/cache paths, but current `origin/main` still has this single-row metadata-context gap.

Related: #92057, #90814, #90819, #86788, #86794, #88166, #75013.

## Why

Local isolated investigation found a strong limit-specific split:

- `sessions.list` with `agentId=main`, `activeMinutes=60`, `limit=1`: median ~8196 ms
- Same request with `limit=2`: median ~41.55 ms

An isolated patched rehearsal of the generated runtime chunk changed the `limit=1` median to ~41.8 ms, matching the `limit=2` path. This indicated the issue was the single-row rowContext branch, not session cleanup or general CLI overhead.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- Guarded focused gateway Vitest:

```bash
CI=1 corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/session-utils.single-row-cache.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --no-file-parallelism --reporter=default --testTimeout=30000
```

Result:

- 3 test files passed
- 12 tests passed
- duration 18.04s on the fresh `origin/main` branch

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions.list` single-row safe-list requests no longer skip the request-scoped metadata context and fall back to uncached subagent metadata lookups.

Real setup tested: disposable/stage OpenClaw source checkout based on current `origin/main`; no live user gateway, live config, or live state mutation was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/session-utils.single-row-cache.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --no-file-parallelism --reporter=default --testTimeout=30000`.

Evidence after fix: focused regression passes across gateway-core, gateway-server, and gateway-client projects; isolated patched-runtime rehearsal reduced the observed `limit=1` median from ~8196 ms to ~41.8 ms.

Observed result after fix: `listSessionsFromStore()` and `listSessionsFromStoreAsync()` both build the shared subagent metadata context for `limit: 1` and avoid the uncached child-session display lookup fallback in the regression test.

What was not tested: a live production gateway was not patched or restarted; broader full-suite gateway regression was not run in this PR pass.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Test
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Breaking change

## Summary
- Cache shared `/models` provider data so Telegram and Discord reuse the same in-flight build instead of repeating the expensive browse/auth work for every callback.
- Keep the earlier bounded browse catalog loading and auth-discovery reduction in the same PR.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker.test.ts`
- `openclaw models list --provider litellm --json` on the Homebrew-installed binary

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: Telegram `/models`, provider selection, and model selection are measurably faster after the shared `/models` cache patch and the Homebrew hotfix that was loaded after the restart.
- Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw setup on macOS with the Homebrew-installed `openclaw` binary; gateway was restarted at `13:12:37` before the faster run.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: In Telegram, run `/models`, choose a provider, then choose a model. Compare the before/after screenshots from the same Telegram flow.
- Evidence after fix: Screenshot `截圖 2026-06-12 14.09.05.png` shows `00:20.16` total for the three Telegram stages after the fix. Screenshot `截圖 2026-06-12 03.44.05.png` shows `00:44.10` total before the fix.

- What was not tested: Cross-platform timing reruns on Windows x64 and Linux arm64 in this proof step.

target.title: perf(models-config): unify auth-profile fingerprint cache + targetProvider short-circuit
target.body:
## Summary

Supersedes #73260 and #73261 with one reconciled implementation. The two are **complementary, not redundant** — both avoid the expensive `resolveImplicitProviders` path (plugin discovery fan-out + live provider-catalog fetches), layered in `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` as: in-process whole-input **fingerprint cache** (warm-process memoizer) → provider-scoped **structural short-circuit** (cold-start / cross-provider-change skip) → full plan.

Reconciled here:

- **#73260** — content-hash auth-profile fingerprint + `models.json` drift detection. Warm-process whole-input memoizer; OAuth token rotation no longer busts the cache.
- **#73261** — provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit with disk-vs-config validation. Skips discovery on cold start / gateway restart, or when an *unrelated* provider changed.

Plus a correctness fix shared by both PRs, and carried refinements:

- **Fingerprint the canonical SQLite auth store** (`openclaw-agent.sqlite`, via the warm pooled handle) instead of the legacy `auth-profiles.json` file. Both prior PRs hashed the legacy JSON, which reads `absent` for every agent already migrated to SQLite — so real credential changes never invalidated the implicit-provider-discovery cache. Reading the canonical store fixes that, removes the symlink / oversize-file attack surface (it's a DB read, not a file read), and aligns with the "SQLite only / no legacy runtime readers" storage policy. Volatile OAuth session fields are still stripped before hashing, so token rotation stays cache-stable.
- **Per-type token-expiry volatile fields** — `type:"token"` profiles keep `expires*` in the hash so an expired static token invalidates the cache, while OAuth session rotation does not.
- **Plugin-catalog wiring** — merge generated plugin catalogs into the pre-plan parsed `models.json` and write them in the skip / noop / write paths.

All security hardenings from both PRs are preserved (fail-closed `uncacheable`, prototype-pollution guards, bounded compare depth, per-model transport refusal, validated-outcome TOCTOU close, scoped-cache isolation).

## Review feedback (round 1 — Codex/Clawsweeper durable review)

Both blocking findings from the durable review (verdict: *needs changes + needs real behavior proof*, head `1621b52177`) are addressed in commit `e1524762ec`:

- **[P1] Validate plugin catalog sidecars before warm cache returns** (`src/agents/models-config.ts:1501-1504`). The branch had moved drift detection to a content outcome on the cache entry but only captured root `models.json`, leaving the generated plugin catalog sidecars (`plugins/<plugin>/catalog.json`, written by the planner and consumed by `ModelRegistry.loadCustomModels`) unvalidated — a sidecar deleted/tampered after a warm entry was cached would still hit the cache and skip reconciliation (provider-routing integrity hole). Fixed by adding `readPluginCatalogsContentOutcome` (a `ContentHashOutcome` over the sorted `(relativePath, content-hash)` pairs of every generated sidecar), captured on the ready-cache entry across all write/skip/noop/short-circuit paths and re-checked on every warm hit (global + scoped) alongside `modelsJsonOutcome`. Strictly stronger than main's mtime fingerprint: hashes sidecar *contents* (catches mtime-preserving tamper) and folds the sidecar path set into the digest (catches add/delete). Fail-closed: an `uncacheable` sidecar never compares equal.

- **[P2] Avoid creating the auth database for a fingerprint read** (`src/agents/models-config.ts:295`). `readAuthProfilesStableOutcome` routed through `openAuthProfileDatabase`, which `mkdir`s the agent dir, creates the schema, and registers the DB — a write side effect for a read-only cache-key computation. Fixed by calling `readPersistedAuthProfileStoreRaw(agentDir)` with no DB handle, taking the no-create / `{ readOnly: true }` path. No agent SQLite state is materialized to decide cache usability.

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo:core` → clean
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` → clean
- `pnpm vitest run` (the 3 acceptance-criteria files: `models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts`, `models-config.write-serialization.test.ts`, `models-config.target-provider-short-circuit.test.ts`) → **112 passed**, including 4 new regression tests pinning sidecar drift + no-DB-create
- `pnpm config:schema:check` → ok
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` → ok
- `pnpm oxlint` (touched files) → clean of new errors (2 pre-existing `no-promise-executor-return` lines are outside this diff)
- `git diff --check` → clean

## Real behavior proof

Real-runtime proof script `scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` drives the real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` against a real on-disk agent directory, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, and the real no-create auth-store read. **No mocking of the seam under test**; only the network edge is avoided (static `apiKey`, no live provider catalog fetch).

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A plugin-catalog sidecar deleted or tampered after a warm models-config cache entry was created still hit the cache and skipped reconciliation (provider-routing integrity hole, [P1]); separately, the read-only auth fingerprint computation created the agent SQLite DB as a write side effect ([P2]). After the fix, sidecar drift busts the warm cache and the rogue file is reconciled away, and the fingerprint read never materializes the auth DB.
- **Real environment tested:** Real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` runtime path driven against an on-disk temp agent directory with real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, and the real no-create auth-store read. Topic worktree at head `e1524762ec`. No mocking of the seam under test; only the network edge is avoided (static apiKey, no live provider fetch).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` (equivalently `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts`), run from the topic worktree.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output (stdout) from the live run of the proof against the real cache code path:

```
[1/3] sidecar-drift-busts-cache-and-reconciles
    rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false
    ok
[2/3] fingerprint-read-does-not-create-agent-db
    auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false
    ok
[3/3] stable-no-sidecar-still-hits
    models.json mtimeMs cold=1780794326032.3853 warm=1780794326032.3853 ino cold=3879005 warm=3879005
    ok

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All three runtime assertions passed (process exit 0). Scenario 1 — the rogue sidecar is absent after re-plan (`rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false`): the warm cache correctly missed on sidecar drift and `removeStalePluginCatalogs` reconciled the tampered file away. Scenario 2 — no `openclaw-agent.sqlite` / `-wal` / `-shm` was created by the fingerprint read (`auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false`): the read-only cache-key computation no longer has a write side effect. Scenario 3 — steady-state no-sidecar inputs still hit the cache (identical `models.json` inode and mtime cold vs warm), confirming no perf regression.
- **What was not tested:** A fully live multi-provider gateway boot is not exercised; the cache populate/drift/reconcile and no-create auth-read paths are driven end-to-end against real on-disk state by the proof script above. Unit coverage (`models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts` + 3 acceptance files, 112 passed) is supplemental.

target.title: feat(claude): add claude-bridge app-server harness extension
target.body:
## Summary

Inspiration: https://openclaw.ai/blog/openai-models-in-openclaw-done-right; achieve parity for Claude

OpenClaw currently has no first-class harness for Anthropic models. Anthropic users either fall back to a generic provider client (no native tool execution, no extended-thinking awareness, no per-turn rate-limit surfacing) or run a Claude harness out-of-tree with no contract for OpenClaw's plugin-SDK seams.

The codex harness already ships in-tree with the same shape we want — `extensions/codex/src/app-server/` is a codex-app-server JSON-RPC client over stdio. We have a working Anthropic-side equivalent built and running in production on the author's setup; opening it for maintainer steer before doing more SDK work on top.

**What is the intended outcome?**

- Bundled extension `@openclaw/claude` registers `claude-bridge` as a harness option in the model picker.
- At turn-start, the extension spawns an `openclaw-claude-bridge` subprocess and speaks the codex-shaped JSON-RPC protocol over stdio, directly wrapping the latest claude agent SDK. The server (currently published as `@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge` — fork-preview path; would migrate to `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` if the maintainers republish it) handles Anthropic API I/O, rate-limit surfacing, image-payload sanitization, and plugin-thread-config persistence.
- Bridge-side handles event projection into OpenClaw's notification/accumulator surface, transcript mirroring with idempotency keys, dynamic-tool aggregate output cap, vision-tools de-duplication, and `/claude` slash commands.
- See `extensions/claude/README.md` for the full feature map.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- Republishing the server under `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` — needs maintainer decision; the `@zeroaltitude` scope dependency is the current placeholder.
- Migrating to OpenClaw's standard plugin-SDK provider hooks beyond what `extensions/codex/` already uses. The bridge follows codex's pattern verbatim where applicable.
- Anything outside the harness bridge surface.

**What does success look like?** Anthropic users get parity with codex users: native tool dispatch, rate-limit-aware errors, extended-thinking-friendly transcript mirroring, harness-level event projection. No regressions to existing harnesses.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

1. **Scope concern — am I taking too much core?** The branch touches `src/plugins/command-registration.ts`, `src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts`, `src/plugins/agent-tool-result-middleware-types.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts`, `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts`, and `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`. Each is small and necessary to wire a harness extension, but the contribution guide says "one thing per PR." Happy to split into prep PRs first if maintainers prefer.
2. **External-plugin vs. bundled-extension** — Per CONTRIBUTING.md "most features should be third-party plugins." Open to packaging this entirely as an external plugin instead of `extensions/claude/`, if that is preferred.
3. **Anthropic-API behavior delegated to a separate server repo** — Most of the actual API logic lives in the server (https://github.com/zeroaltitude/openclaw-plugins/tree/main/openclaw-claude/server). Reviewers will probably want to inspect that companion repo before approving.
4. **`claude-bridge` harness id naming** — there's no precedent for "<vendor>-bridge" harness names; renamable. (The reason I did this initially was to ensure that I wasn't representing that this work was by anthropic -- matters more on the server naming than the in-openclaw extension tho)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Anthropic-model agents previously had no in-tree harness; the bridge unlocks native tool runs against Claude through the same JSON-RPC pattern codex uses.
- **Real environment tested:** Author's openclaw gateway running on Linux (WSL2 / Ubuntu), Node 22.x. Author's agents  are configured with `claude-bridge` as their harness and are in daily active use against `claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-opus-4-7`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `pnpm install && pnpm build`
  2. `openclaw gateway restart --deep`
  3. Send a Slack DM to one of the claude-bridge agents
  4. Agent responds with native Anthropic tools used; transcript file contains the projected events.
  5. `/claude status` in the same thread prints shared-client liveness.
- **Evidence after fix (fresh capture, 2026-05-28):** This gateway runs the claude-bridge harness as the default runtime for all Anthropic models (`agents.defaults.models.*.agentRuntime.id = "claude-bridge"`), so the harness is exercised continuously across multiple agents. Captured live:
  1. **Bridge liveness** — shared-client lifecycle today: `claude-bridge: spawned ×5 / initialized ×5 / process exited ×5` (restarts and re-initializes without manual intervention).
  2. **A turn completing with visible tool/progress output** — one bridge turn dispatching consecutive tools, each completing, with per-tool provenance timing from the observability plugin: `ToolSearch → after_tool_call 1.9ms`, `exec → 1.7ms`, `Write → 1.9ms`, `Agent → 1.9ms`.
  3. **Transcript / result event stream** — the run persists a projected event stream to `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl`: parentId-chained `message` events covering the user turn, assistant `tool_use` blocks, and `tool_result` blocks.
  4. **`/claude status`** (live, from a bridge-backed agent):
     ```
     Claude app-server status
     Shared client: running
     Command: openclaw-claude-bridge
     Last stderr: [turn-runner] system event {"subtype":"init"} / {"subtype":"status"} / unhandled SDK event {"type":"rate_limit_event"}
     ```
  *Log-hygiene note:* a few `claude-bridge: runAttempt failed` lines appear in the same logs; they are not bridge defects — one class is a context-overflow on a zero-message scheduled run (auto-compaction correctly skipped), the other is transient model-call failover during an MCP reconnect; both recovered on the next turn. Earlier daily-driver context still applies: Tabitha (claude-bridge) has been in active production use for ~2 weeks.
- **Observed result after fix:** Anthropic turns complete normally; tool calls fire; rate-limit messages are user-actionable; resume across restart works via the `.claude-binding.json` sidecar.
- **What was not tested:**
  - Windows/macOS gateway hosts (only Linux/WSL2 verified).
  - Bun runtime (Node 22 only).
  - Anthropic enterprise/Bedrock/Vertex API surfaces (only public Anthropic API).
  - Concurrent multi-account scenarios beyond 3 agents.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Author runs a single-host gateway. The server is currently published under the author's `@zeroaltitude` npm scope (fork-preview), so reproducing this requires installing the bridge from that scope until maintainers republish.
- **Before evidence:** N/A — no prior in-tree harness for Anthropic to compare against.

## Tests and validation

- Real world use for over a month on a broad diversity of tasks
- `pnpm build` ✓ (local)
- `pnpm test extensions/claude` ✓ (local — extension unit tests, including event projector, dynamic-tools middleware port, thread fork policy envelope, transcript mirror dedupe)
- `pnpm test:extensions` not run since last rebase — recommend Testbox/Crabbox run before non-draft review.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit per contributor rule; maintainer to add on land.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — new `claude-bridge` harness appears in model picker; new `/claude` slash command surface.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** Yes — new plugin `configSchema` (see `extensions/claude/openclaw.plugin.json`). Existing agent configs unaffected unless they explicitly opt into `claude-bridge` harness. Doctor contract added (`extensions/claude/doctor-contract-api.ts`).
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** Yes (intentional). The bridge spawns a subprocess that holds the Anthropic API key in-memory and makes network calls to Anthropic. Subprocess lifecycle is owned by the harness, not user shell. The plugin SDK enabler changes (e.g. `command-registration.ts`) are additive and follow existing codex-extension precedent.
- **Highest-risk area:** Server-bridge stdio lifecycle and the few SDK enabler edits in `src/plugins/**`. If the SDK changes accidentally relax a guard for non-claude paths, every harness would inherit the change.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The SDK changes are narrowly scoped — each follows a codex-extension-shaped precedent. Happy to factor them into a prep PR if reviewers want to land them separately first.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Maintainer steer on scope: (a) accept as bundled extension as-is, (b) split SDK enablers into a prep PR first, or (c) request packaging as an external plugin.
- **Still waiting on:** Maintainer decision on the above. CI run on this push.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** None yet — opening draft.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted disclosure:** Developed with the assistance of Claude Code (anthropic.com/claude-code). All design decisions, production deployment, and real-behavior verification are human-driven; AI assistance was used for implementation across multiple sessions. Session transcripts available on request.

target.title: feat: add external plugin approval verification
target.body:
## Summary

This is the narrowed AgentKit host API slice after @kevinslin's feedback about avoiding a broad custom-action surface.

- Adds `externalResolution` for plugin approvals as a single command-template field, not arbitrary custom action arrays.
- Keeps core as the normal approval owner: OpenClaw still renders core decisions from `allowedDecisions`, and AgentKit uses only core `deny` there.
- Adds `plugin.approval.resolveVerified` for proof-backed allow resolution, scoped to the originating `pluginId` and registered behind `operator.admin`.
- Teaches approval view models, text/channel renderers, Control UI parsing, protocol schema, docs, and SDK types to carry/render the narrow external verification command.
- Rejects mixed normal allow decisions with external verification, so allow-once/allow-always must go through the verified plugin-owned resolver.

This replaces the earlier broader approach from #82431. The chat/status-card follow-up from #82471 is not required for the current AgentKit end-to-end path.

## Review context

This PR is the narrower replacement for the earlier custom-action approach. It does **not** add arbitrary plugin-defined approval actions.

The concrete use case is the external AgentKit plugin:

- https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit
- https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit/pull/2
- https://clawhub.ai/plugins/@guardiola31337/agentkit

AgentKit needs to let a user complete external World verification before a protected OpenClaw tool continues, while keeping OpenClaw core as the approval owner.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Agent calls protected tool] --> B[OpenClaw creates plugin approval]
  B --> C[OpenClaw renders normal approval prompt]
  C --> D[User chooses external verification command]
  D --> E[AgentKit starts World verification]
  E --> F[World proof succeeds]
  F --> G[AgentKit calls resolveVerified]
  G --> H[OpenClaw checks plugin ownership]
  H --> I[Tool call continues]
```

## Trust boundary

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph Core[OpenClaw core]
    A[Owns pending approval]
    B[Renders normal deny and timeout handling]
    C[Validates approval id]
    D[Checks originating plugin id]
    E[Resolves approval]
  end

  subgraph Plugin[AgentKit plugin]
    F[Owns World verification]
    G[Shows QR/link]
    H[Submits proof-backed allow]
  end

  A --> F
  H --> D
  D --> E
```

## Why this shape

The earlier approach exposed arbitrary `actions`, which made every approval-capable client understand plugin-defined buttons. That was too broad for this use case.

This PR keeps the core approval contract intact and adds only:

- `externalResolution`: a narrow command-template hint for external verification.
- `plugin.approval.resolveVerified`: an ownership-checked resolver so plugin X can resolve only approvals created by plugin X.

Core still owns pending approval state, timeout, deny handling, and normal approval rendering. AgentKit owns only the external World verification step and can only resolve approvals that originated from AgentKit.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts src/infra/approval-view-model.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts extensions/slack/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/build-all.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json --declaration true`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: external HITL plugins can ask OpenClaw to show a narrow verification command for allow decisions while core keeps denial and pending approval ownership.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 using the gateway/plugin approval tests, channel renderer tests, Control UI tests, full build, SDK API check, and a companion AgentKit plugin gateway e2e run against this host API.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the commands listed in `Verification`, plus `pnpm test:openclaw-hitl` in the companion AgentKit plugin checkout linked to this OpenClaw host API branch.

Evidence after fix: copied after-fix output from the AgentKit local OpenClaw gateway e2e run:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "denyApprovalCommands": [
    "/agentkit approve plugin:ad654b79-39c5-4819-9d35-a15a828bbd39 allow-once",
    "/agentkit approve plugin:ad654b79-39c5-4819-9d35-a15a828bbd39 allow-always",
    "/approve plugin:ad654b79-39c5-4819-9d35-a15a828bbd39 deny"
  ],
  "deniedHookResult": {
    "blocked": true,
    "kind": "failure",
    "deniedReason": "plugin-approval",
    "reason": "Denied by user",
    "params": {
      "cmd": "echo agentkit"
    }
  },
  "allowApprovalCommands": [
    "/agentkit approve plugin:731c8300-5d34-4a97-883f-2696cbb2e2b8 allow-once",
    "/agentkit approve plugin:731c8300-5d34-4a97-883f-2696cbb2e2b8 allow-always",
    "/approve plugin:731c8300-5d34-4a97-883f-2696cbb2e2b8 deny"
  ],
  "allowedHookResult": {
    "blocked": false,
    "params": {
      "cmd": "echo agentkit-allow"
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: plugin approval prompts render external verification as command text, core deny remains available, verified allow resolution is admin-scoped and plugin-owned, and reaction/manual fallback prompts no longer claim `allow-always` is unavailable when external verification exposes it.

What was not tested: a live World App QR scan from the TUI in this OpenClaw PR branch. The companion AgentKit plugin branch covers the plugin-level HITL and local OpenClaw gateway e2e path.

target.title: fix(memory): preserve keyword-only results in hybrid search when chunk IDs don't overlap (fixes #92337)
target.body:
## Related

Fixes #92337

## Summary

When keyword and vector search return non-overlapping chunk IDs (common with CJK/trigram queries), keyword-only results were silently dropped during post-merge filtering. The existing fallback in `MemoryIndexManager.searchHybrid()` only triggered when ALL merged results were below `minScore` — if even one vector result passed the threshold, keyword-only results were discarded entirely, leaving `textScore: 0` in the output.

**Root cause:** `manager.ts:816` — `strict.length > 0 || keywordResults.length === 0` short-circuits the keyword fallback whenever any vector result passes `minScore`.

**Fix:** Always include keyword-only results (entries where `vectorScore === 0`) alongside strict vector+keyword matches, deduplicated and sorted by combined score.

## Changes

- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts` — Replace the conditional keyword fallback with unconditional inclusion of keyword-only results
- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts` — Add test for non-overlapping chunk ID scenario

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** mergeHybridResults correctly preserves keyword-only results when chunk IDs don't overlap with vector results
- **Environment tested:** macOS 26.4.1, Node.js v22.16.0, pnpm 11.2.2, OpenClaw local checkout at commit `9c754236`
- **Steps run after the patch:**
  1. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` — deps up to date
  2. `pnpm build` — full project build succeeds
  3. Verified the fix logic by inspecting `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts` lines 814-854: the conditional `strict.length > 0 || keywordResults.length === 0` was replaced with unconditional inclusion of keyword-only entries (`vectorScore === 0`)
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  $ pnpm build
  ✓ built in 546ms
  [build-all] ui:build done in 1.42s
  [build-all] phase timings: total 117.3s

  $ grep -n "keywordOnly" extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts
  837:    const keywordOnly = merged.filter(
  838:      (entry) =>
  839:        keywordKeys.has(`${entry.source}:${entry.path}:${entry.startLine}:${entry.endLine}`) &&
  840:        entry.vectorScore === 0,
  841:    );
  842:    const combined = [...strict, ...keywordOnly];
  ```
  The `combined` array merges strict vector+keyword results with keyword-only results, deduplicates by composite key, and sorts by score descending.
- **Observed result after fix:** The build completes successfully. The code now unconditionally preserves keyword-only results (entries where `vectorScore === 0` and valid `textScore`) alongside vector+keyword matches, even when chunk IDs don't overlap.
- **What was not tested:** Live CJK memory search with real embeddings (requires Ollama + indexed memory files); the fix is verified at the build and code-inspection level covering the exact merge and filtering logic

target.title: perf(qmd): persistent export-state cache + stat fast path in exportSessions
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** For agents with thousands of session transcripts (Eddie's main: 4,052 JSONL files / 158 MB), `qmdManager.exportSessions()` was the dominant gateway CPU consumer. Every interval (5 min) and every boot, it walked every session file and called `buildSessionEntry` per file — full JSONL read + parse + redaction + hashing + render — even when nothing had changed.
- **Why it matters:** The existing skip mechanism (`exportedSessionState` in-memory `Map`) gated only the actual Markdown write, not the upstream work; and the `Map` didn't survive restart. Result: every 5-minute interval triggered a full re-walk + re-render cycle, plus a cold start every gateway boot.
- **What changed:**
  1. Persist the per-file state cache to `<qmdDir>/sessions/.export-state.json` alongside a `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` sentinel and the resolved `exportDir`. Loaded lazily on the first `exportSessions()` call (so it works in both `full` and `status` init modes). Atomic write via `writeFileWithinRoot`. Schema / renderVersion / exportDir mismatch → drop cache cleanly.
  2. Add a stat-based fast path before `buildSessionEntry`. If the source JSONL's `(size, mtimeMs)` match the cached entry, skip `buildSessionEntry` entirely and trust the existing export. The slow path still runs on any stat change and remains responsible for hashing, redacting, and producing the markdown qmd consumes.
- **Scope boundary:** `renderSessionMarkdown`, `redactSensitiveText`, line wrapping, and the markdown qmd consumes are unchanged. `buildSessionEntry` itself is unchanged on the slow path. The persisted cache is per-`exportDir` and invalidates cleanly on `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` bumps.

**Effect on Eddie's main agent (4,052 sessions / 158 MB):**
- Boot qmd sync: **312s → single-digit seconds**
- Interval qmd sync: **38s → ~50-500 ms**
- The every-5-min CPU spike + GC tail collapses

## Linked issue

- Closes openclaw-ys0 (Beads tracking issue)

## Test coverage

New tests cover correctness of the cache contract:

- Coverage level: Unit test (added two cases to `qmd-manager.test.ts`)
- Target test or file: `extensions/qmd/qmd-manager.test.ts` (`load+persist roundtrip` and `exportDir mismatch invalidation`)
- Scenario the tests lock in:
  1. State cache loads from disk on first call, persists changes, survives a fresh manager construction.
  2. `exportDir` mismatch (for example, a config change) drops the persisted cache and re-derives state.

Existing 102 tests still pass.

## Tradeoffs / known caveats

- A `touch -d <original-mtime>` rewrite of a JSONL would be missed by the stat fast path until another size/mtime-changing event. **Not introduced here** — the previous skip-write-on-hash-match already swallowed same-content rewrites; this PR doesn't make that surface worse.
- **`SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` bump required when changing** `renderSessionMarkdown`, `redactSensitiveText`, line wrapping, or anything else that affects the bytes written to `qmd/sessions/*.md`. Documented inline at the constant declaration.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The QMD session-export cache must avoid rebuilding unchanged session markdown on repeated syncs, while still rebuilding a session export when the cached markdown target has been deleted but the source JSONL size/mtime still match the persisted `.export-state.json` entry.

**Real environment tested:** Real `QmdMemoryManager` from the topic branch (`perf/qmd-export-stat-fast-path` at `2a92f91478`) with `qmd 2.1.0` available on PATH, driven by a Node/tsx script against an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under `/tmp`. The driver constructed the production manager and called `manager.sync({ reason: "manual" })`; it did not use Vitest, `vi.mock`, or spawn fakes.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the real-driver command from the topic worktree:

```text
$ ./node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/.../qmd-export-driver.mjs
```

The driver executed four phases: first boot export, second boot with unchanged inputs, manual deletion of `session-1.md` while `.export-state.json` survived, then third boot to prove the missing cached target is rebuilt.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the real-driver run in PR comment 4384871421:

```text
=== Phase 1: first boot — expect markdown written ===
-- boot 1 --
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=manual durationMs=429
  sync done in 445 ms
export file size: 49 bytes; contains 'hello tank-real-proof': true
export mtimeMs: 1778038392470.7026
cache version: 1  entries: 1  target = undefined

=== Phase 2: second boot, same inputs — expect FAST PATH (no rewrite) ===
-- boot 2 --
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=manual durationMs=313
  sync done in 315 ms
export mtimeMs after boot 2: 1778038392470.7026  (== boot 1 mtime? true )

=== Phase 3: delete session-1.md while .export-state.json cache survives ===
session-1.md exists? false  (cache file still here: true )

=== Phase 4: third boot — fast-path access check must fall through and rebuild ===
-- boot 3 --
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=manual durationMs=384
  sync done in 387 ms
rebuilt export size: 49 bytes; contains 'hello tank-real-proof': true
rebuilt mtimeMs: 1778038393222.7556  (> boot 1 mtime? true )
```

**Observed result after fix:** The second boot preserved the exact markdown `mtimeMs`, proving the unchanged-input fast path skipped the rewrite. After deleting the markdown export, the third boot rebuilt it and restored the expected content (`hello tank-real-proof`) with a newer `mtimeMs`, proving the missing-target regression path falls through to the slow rebuild instead of silently trusting stale cache state.

**What was not tested:** A thousands-session timing benchmark on Eddie's full 4,052-session corpus was not rerun for this proof. The live driver proves the behavioral cache invariants; the original timing numbers remain observational context from the production-sized main-agent workload.

target.title: fix(logging): support root log file overrides
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw had a root `--log-level` override, but no matching root `--log-file` override, and the logger/status helpers did not honor `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE`
- Why it matters: multi-instance or service deployments need per-process log isolation without rewriting shared config files just to move one process's file sink
- What changed: added root `--log-file <path>`, propagated it into `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE` during CLI preaction, taught logger/path helpers to honor that env override, and documented the override path
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not add log rotation, does not mutate `logging.file` in config, and does not change file-log retention or max-size behavior

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #61295
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the logger already honored config-backed `logging.file`, but the root CLI startup path only promoted `--log-level`, not `--log-file`, and the shared log-path helpers had no `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE` override layer
- Missing detection / guardrail: there were no tests covering root log-file option propagation, env log-file override resolution, or daemon status reporting when the file sink was overridden out-of-band
- Contributing context (if known): several CLI/status/docs surfaces already treated log-file path as configurable, but they were only looking at config/default paths instead of the full effective override stack

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/logging/logger-env.test.ts`, `src/cli/program/preaction.test.ts`, `src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: root `--log-file` should become `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE`, logger resolution should prefer that env path, and daemon status should report the same effective file sink
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it validates the three boundaries that need to agree for users to trust the override: CLI parsing, runtime logger resolution, and status/reporting
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing root-option tests covered `--log-level`, but not file-path overrides
- If no new test is added, why not:

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `openclaw --log-file /path/to/openclaw.log ...` now forces the file log sink for that process.
- `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE=/path/to/openclaw.log` now overrides `logging.file`.
- Daemon status/reporting now shows the effective env-overridden log file path instead of only the config/default path.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[root CLI invocation]
  -> [--log-level handled]
  -> [--log-file ignored]
  -> [logger/status fall back to config/default path]

After:
[root CLI invocation with --log-file]
  -> [preaction exports OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE]
  -> [logger + status helpers prefer env log path]
  -> [runtime and reported file sink stay aligned]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local pnpm/vitest
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): root CLI + logger bootstrap + daemon status
- Relevant config (redacted): default config with per-process log file override via root CLI flag or `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE`

### Steps

1. Run `openclaw --log-file /tmp/custom-openclaw.log status` or export `OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE=/tmp/custom-openclaw.log`.
2. Start or inspect a gateway/CLI command that writes file logs.
3. Check the resolved log file path from runtime/status surfaces.

### Expected

- The process should write logs to `/tmp/custom-openclaw.log`, and status helpers should report that same path.

### Actual

- Before this change, there was no root `--log-file` path, and env-based log-file overrides were not part of the resolved logger/status path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: targeted vitest run for `src/logging/logger-env.test.ts`, `src/cli/program/preaction.test.ts`, `src/cli/program/help.test.ts`, `src/infra/cli-root-options.test.ts`, `src/cli/argv.test.ts`, `src/cli/program/route-args.test.ts`, `src/cli/program/routes.test.ts`, `src/cli/run-main.test.ts`, and `src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts`
- Edge cases checked: root option parsing with separate and `=` forms, env override precedence, Vitest silent-file fast path with explicit env file, daemon status surfacing the effective path
- What you did **not** verify: full repo-wide `pnpm check` / `pnpm test`, a live manual CLI run outside the test harness, or log rotation behavior beyond the existing code path

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: another surface could still report only config/default log paths and drift from runtime
  - Mitigation: `resolveConfiguredLogFilePath()` now includes the env override and daemon status has a dedicated regression test
- Risk: root-option parsing could regress fast-path commands that skip bootstrap work
  - Mitigation: argv/root-option/run-main route tests were extended to cover `--log-file` alongside the existing `--log-level` path

AI-assisted: Yes (Codex). Testing: targeted vitest coverage listed above. I attempted `codex review --base origin/main`, but the local Codex CLI is currently rate-limited.

target.title: feat(plugins): add session followup turn API and gateway-restart extension
target.body:
## Summary

Add a new `runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn()` method to the plugin runtime, enabling plugins to schedule proactive agent turns for any session — including cold sessions with no active user interaction.

> **Supersedes #60951** — rebased onto latest main, all CI issues resolved, bug fixes included.

## Problem

Plugins have no way to inject a user-role message into an existing session and trigger an agent turn. The available primitives (`enqueueSystemEvent`, `subagent.run`) either don't initiate turns or create new sessions instead of continuing the original conversation. This blocks use cases like:

- **Post-restart session resume** — an agent restarts its own gateway and needs to automatically continue working after the restart completes
- **Model backend switching** — an agent swaps inference backends (e.g., switching between local LLMs on a single-GPU workstation) and needs to resume the session on the new model with full context
- **Proactive plugin notifications** — a service detects an external event and needs to wake a sleeping session
- **Deferred task completion** — a plugin schedules follow-up work after a long-running background operation

## Solution

### Core API: `runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn()`

A new `followup` namespace on `PluginRuntimeCore` that:

1. **Reconstructs a full `FollowupRun`** from the session store via `buildFollowupRunForSession()` — resolving agent identity, workspace, provider/model, session file, and delivery context from the persisted `SessionEntry`.

2. **Enqueues the run** into the existing followup queue infrastructure with preserved queue mode (respects `mode` of any existing queue for the session key, defaults to `"followup"`).

3. **Handles cold-start drain** by detecting whether the session's followup queue is already draining. If not (cold session), creates a fresh followup runner with preloaded session metadata and a no-op typing controller, then kicks the drain loop.

```typescript
// Plugin usage
const enqueued = await api.runtime.followup.enqueueFollowupTurn({
  sessionKey: "agent:my-agent:telegram:direct:12345",
  prompt: "Background task complete. Here are the results...",
  source: "my-plugin",
});
```

### Bundled Extension: `gateway-restart`

A reference implementation that demonstrates the followup turn API:

- **Tool** (`gateway_restart`): Agent calls this to run optional allowlisted pre-commands, write a restart marker (only after commands succeed), then exit the process. Systemd (or equivalent) auto-restarts the gateway.

- **Service** (`gateway-restart-watcher`): On startup, checks for the restart marker. If found, calls `enqueueFollowupTurn()` to resume the original session with a completion message. Marker is deleted only after successful enqueue — preserved for retry on failure.

Disabled by default (`enabledByDefault: false`). Opt-in via `plugins.entries.gateway-restart.enabled: true`.

## Files Changed

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/build-followup-run.ts` | New: cold-session FollowupRun builder with session store resolution |
| `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-followup.ts` | New: plugin runtime followup namespace with hot/cold session detection |
| `src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts` | Add `followup` type to `PluginRuntimeCore` |
| `src/plugins/runtime/index.ts` | Wire `createRuntimeFollowup()` |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/index.ts` | New: gateway-restart plugin (tool + watcher service) |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/openclaw.plugin.json` | New: plugin manifest |
| `extensions/gateway-restart/package.json` | New: package metadata |
| `test/helpers/plugins/plugin-runtime-mock.ts` | Add `followup` to mock |
| `**/**.test.ts` | 24 unit tests across 3 test files |

## Bug Fixes (vs #60951)

1. **Marker written after pre-commands** — prevents stale markers when a pre-command fails
2. **Marker deleted after enqueue confirmed** — preserves marker for retry if `enqueueFollowupTurn()` returns false
3. **Hot session guard** — `rememberFollowupDrainCallback` only registers for cold sessions; existing hot-session drain runners are not overwritten
4. **Queue mode preservation** — `enqueueFollowupRun` respects existing queue mode instead of forcing `"followup"`
5. **Session metadata preload** — cold followup runners receive preloaded session store and entry for accounting/refresh paths

## Testing

- 24 unit tests across 3 test files (all passing)
- `pnpm check` passes (tsgo + oxlint + all architecture checks)
- Rebased onto latest main
- End-to-end verified on a local workstation running OpenClaw 2026.4.8 with Gemma 4 26B via llama.cpp

## Use Cases This Enables

This API is the foundation for several plugins currently in development:

- **`model-switch`** — seamless switching between local LLMs on single-GPU workstations, with session continuation via `enqueueFollowupTurn()` after the new model is healthy
- **`inference-guard`** — priority-aware request scheduling for single-slot inference (heartbeat/cron defer behind user messages)
- **Self-modifying agents** — agents that update their own config, restart the gateway, and validate changes automatically

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm check` passes
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run extensions/gateway-restart/index.test.ts` — 6 tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run src/plugins/runtime/runtime-followup.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/queue/build-followup-run.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [ ] Gateway-restart tool writes marker only after pre-commands succeed
- [ ] Watcher service enqueues followup turn and deletes marker on success
- [ ] Watcher preserves marker on enqueue failure

## AI Disclosure

This PR was developed with AI assistance (Claude Code). Testing level: **fully tested** — 24 unit tests, `pnpm check` green, end-to-end verified on local hardware. The author understands all code in this PR and can explain design decisions.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(agents): treat NO_REPLY-only turns as silent regardless of silence flag (#92038)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92038.

When the assistant replies with only the `NO_REPLY` token and no deliverable payloads, the embedded runner was treating it as an empty response. This triggered:

1. Empty-response retry with visible-answer continuation
2. Model-fallback cascade (`reason=format`) when retries also returned empty

This happened even though the assistant intentionally chose silence, particularly on heartbeat triggers where `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` defaults to `false`.

## Changes

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts`: Added an early check in `shouldTreatEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` to recognize deliberate silent-reply tokens (e.g. `NO_REPLY`) before consulting the `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` configuration flag.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`: Added unit tests covering:
  - `NO_REPLY`-only turns are treated as silent even when `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` is `false`
  - `NO_REPLY` is **not** treated as silent when actual payloads are present

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts --run`: 120 tests passed (including 2 new ones)

## Behavior addressed

- Prevents unnecessary empty-response retries on heartbeat triggers when the assistant intentionally stays silent
- Prevents model-fallback cascades triggered by `format` classification of intentionally empty payloads

target.title: Gate private QQBot group commands
target.body:
## Summary

- Add QQBot-local command visibility classification for OpenClaw core slash commands.
- Add per-group `commandLevel` config alongside `requireMention`:
  - `all`: allow all core slash commands and group-safe QQBot plugin slash commands in the group.
  - `safety`: default behavior; keep safe/group-session commands usable and reject sensitive private-only commands.
  - `strict`: only allow core `/new` and `/reset` in the group.
- Reject private-only core slash commands in QQ groups after ingress access and before mention gating can silently skip them, with a private-chat-only message.
- Reuse the same private-chat-only message for QQBot plugin slash commands marked `c2cOnly`.

Example:

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "qqbot": {
      "groups": {
        "GROUP_OPENID": {
          "requireMention": false,
          "commandLevel": "safety"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Existing `groups.<id>.requireMention` config remains valid; omitting `commandLevel` defaults to `safety`.

## Command levels

### `all`

Allows every known OpenClaw core command in QQ groups:

`/help`, `/commands`, `/tools`, `/skill`, `/status`, `/goal`, `/diagnostics`, `/crestodian`, `/tasks`, `/allowlist`, `/approve`, `/context`, `/btw`, `/side`, `/export-session`, `/export`, `/export-trajectory`, `/trajectory`, `/tts`, `/whoami`, `/id`, `/session`, `/subagents`, `/acp`, `/focus`, `/unfocus`, `/agents`, `/steer`, `/tell`, `/config`, `/mcp`, `/plugins`, `/plugin`, `/debug`, `/usage`, `/stop`, `/restart`, `/activation`, `/send`, `/new`, `/reset`, `/compact`, `/think`, `/thinking`, `/t`, `/verbose`, `/v`, `/trace`, `/fast`, `/reasoning`, `/reason`, `/elevated`, `/elev`, `/exec`, `/model`, `/models`, `/queue`, `/bash`

Allows these QQBot plugin commands in QQ groups:

`/bot-help`, `/bot-ping`

Blocks these QQBot plugin commands with the private-chat-only message:

`/bot-me`, `/bot-version`, `/bot-upgrade`, `/bot-logs`, `/bot-clear-storage`, `/bot-streaming`, `/bot-approve`, `/bot-group-allways`

Unknown slash text still follows the existing routing path.

### `safety` default

Allows these OpenClaw core commands in QQ groups:

`/help`, `/btw`, `/side`, `/stop`, `/goal`, `/usage`, `/activation`, `/send`, `/new`, `/reset`, `/compact`, `/think`, `/thinking`, `/t`, `/fast`, `/reasoning`, `/reason`, `/queue`

Allows these QQBot plugin commands in QQ groups:

`/bot-help`, `/bot-ping`

Blocks with the private-chat-only message:

`/commands`, `/tools`, `/skill`, `/status`, `/diagnostics`, `/crestodian`, `/tasks`, `/allowlist`, `/approve`, `/context`, `/export-session`, `/export`, `/export-trajectory`, `/trajectory`, `/tts`, `/whoami`, `/id`, `/session`, `/subagents`, `/acp`, `/focus`, `/unfocus`, `/agents`, `/steer`, `/tell`, `/config`, `/mcp`, `/plugins`, `/plugin`, `/debug`, `/restart`, `/verbose`, `/v`, `/trace`, `/elevated`, `/elev`, `/exec`, `/model`, `/models`, `/bash`

Blocks these QQBot plugin commands with the private-chat-only message:

`/bot-me`, `/bot-version`, `/bot-upgrade`, `/bot-logs`, `/bot-clear-storage`, `/bot-streaming`, `/bot-approve`, `/bot-group-allways`

### `strict`

Allows only these OpenClaw core commands in QQ groups:

`/new`, `/reset`

Blocks with the private-chat-only message:

Every other known OpenClaw core command.

Blocks every known QQBot plugin command in QQ groups:

`/bot-help`, `/bot-me`, `/bot-ping`, `/bot-version`, `/bot-upgrade`, `/bot-logs`, `/bot-clear-storage`, `/bot-streaming`, `/bot-approve`, `/bot-group-allways`

## Command descriptions

- `/help`: show available command help.
- `/commands`: list slash commands.
- `/tools`: list available runtime tools.
- `/skill`: run a named skill.
- `/status`: show current runtime status.
- `/goal`: show or control the current agent task goal.
- `/diagnostics`: explain Gateway diagnostics and Codex feedback upload options.
- `/crestodian`: run the Crestodian setup and repair helper.
- `/tasks`: list background tasks for this session.
- `/allowlist`: list, add, or remove allowlist entries.
- `/approve`: approve or deny exec requests.
- `/context`: explain how context is built and used.
- `/btw`, `/side`: ask a side question without changing future session context.
- `/export-session`, `/export`: export the current session to HTML.
- `/export-trajectory`, `/trajectory`: export a JSONL trajectory bundle.
- `/tts`: control text-to-speech.
- `/whoami`, `/id`: show the sender id.
- `/session`: manage session-level settings.
- `/subagents`: inspect subagent runs.
- `/acp`: manage ACP sessions and runtime options.
- `/focus`: bind the current thread/topic/conversation to a session target.
- `/unfocus`: remove the current binding.
- `/agents`: list thread-bound agents for this session.
- `/steer`, `/tell`: send guidance to the active run.
- `/config`: show or set config values.
- `/mcp`: show or set OpenClaw MCP servers.
- `/plugins`, `/plugin`: list, show, enable, or disable plugins.
- `/debug`: set runtime debug overrides.
- `/usage`: control usage footer or cost summary.
- `/stop`: stop the current run.
- `/restart`: restart OpenClaw.
- `/activation`: set group activation mode.
- `/send`: set send policy.
- `/new`: start a new session.
- `/reset`: reset the current session.
- `/compact`: compact session context.
- `/think`, `/thinking`, `/t`: set thinking level.
- `/verbose`, `/v`: toggle verbose mode.
- `/trace`: toggle plugin trace lines.
- `/fast`: toggle fast mode.
- `/reasoning`, `/reason`: toggle reasoning visibility.
- `/elevated`, `/elev`: toggle elevated mode.
- `/exec`: set exec defaults for this session.
- `/model`: show or set the model.
- `/models`: list model providers/models.
- `/queue`: adjust queue settings.
- `/bash`: run host shell commands.
- `/bot-help`: show QQBot built-in commands.
- `/bot-me`: show the current QQ sender account id.
- `/bot-ping`: test OpenClaw host to QQ latency.
- `/bot-version`: show OpenClaw framework and QQBot plugin versions.
- `/bot-upgrade`: show QQBot upgrade guidance.
- `/bot-logs`: export recent local OpenClaw logs.
- `/bot-clear-storage`: clean QQBot-generated downloaded files.
- `/bot-streaming`: view or toggle QQBot streaming messages.
- `/bot-approve`: manage exec approval config.
- `/bot-group-allways`: change group default response mode.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/config.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/config/group.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/commands/command-visibility.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/commands/slash-commands-impl.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/commands/framework-registration.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/commands/command-visibility.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/stages/group-gate-stage.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/commands/slash-command-handler.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/qqbot/src/engine/config/group.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/config/group.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/config-schema.ts extensions/qqbot/src/config.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/commands/command-visibility.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/commands/command-visibility.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/commands/slash-commands.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/commands/slash-command-handler.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/commands/slash-commands-impl.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/commands/framework-context-adapter.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/commands/framework-registration.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/commands/framework-registration.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/inbound-context.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/stages/group-gate-stage.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/stages/envelope-stage.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway.ts extensions/qqbot/src/types.ts extensions/qqbot/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

target.title: telegram: support local file-style send inputs with focused attachment fixes
target.body:
# Telegram Attachment PR Body (Final)

## Summary

This PR takes the **focused Option A** approach for Telegram attachment/file-send support.

It improves the existing Telegram attachment/file-send path, aligns local-file-style inputs with the already-existing outbound media pipeline, and includes only the directly related fixes needed for that path to work cleanly in practice.

## Related issues
- Primary feature/contract issue: `#60001`
- Implementation/rollout context: `#56404`
- Adjacent background only (not core scope): `#59970`, `#60156`
- Strategy background only if needed: `#56402`

## What this PR changes

- aligns Telegram local-file-style send inputs with the existing outbound media pipeline
- supports the intended Telegram attachment/file-send path without redesigning the broader messaging contract
- includes only the directly related fixes required for that path to work cleanly
- adds or updates focused tests for the supported path
- documents the supported path, the existing allowed local media-root boundary, and known limitations

## What this PR does not do

- redesign the shared outbound payload contract
- introduce a first-class provider-neutral attachment object system
- introduce a new workspace-relative-only public attachment contract
- land a broad cross-provider attachment abstraction
- bundle unrelated Telegram cleanup or broader chat/workflow redesign

## Validation

### Passed
- `extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts`
  - passed
  - confirms `filePath` / `path` alias handling and `asDocument` behavior
- `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`
  - passed
- `extensions/telegram/src/channel.test.ts`
  - passed
- `extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`
  - passed

### Code-reading / behavior confirmation
- Telegram outbound media/file-send capability already exists in substantial form.
- The recent patch primarily closes the action-surface gap by routing `filePath` / `path` into the existing media send path.
- Shared outbound payloads still appear centered on `mediaUrl/mediaUrls`, which is consistent with the focused Option A scope.
- Boundary wording has been aligned to current code reality: this PR relies on the existing allowed local media-root policy rather than introducing a new workspace-relative-only contract.

### Remaining local validation gap
A fully clean source-CLI end-to-end dry-run / local smoke test has not yet been completed in this source checkout.

The known blocker is currently understood as a separate local source-runtime / shared-bundle issue rather than a Telegram patch failure. Previously recorded findings point to:
- `Multiple matrix-js-sdk entrypoints detected!`

Because of that, the local source-CLI dry-run limitation should be treated as an environment/runtime validation blocker, not as evidence that this Telegram attachment patch itself is failing.

## Reviewer guidance

Please review this as a **focused Telegram attachment/file-send PR with directly related fixes**.

The main review lens should be:
- does the Telegram attachment/file-send path work cleanly?
- do local-file-style inputs correctly reach the existing send pipeline?
- do the included changes stay tightly scoped?
- does the PR avoid turning into a broader messaging redesign?

## Follow-ups

Potential follow-up work, if still wanted later:
- richer provider-neutral attachment object contract
- normalized shared attachment-send error model
- broader Telegram cleanup outside the directly required send-path fixes
- wider cross-provider attachment abstraction only if later proven necessary

target.title: #92241: Gateway holds stale module import paths after update/rollback — inbound messages silently dropped (ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND)
target.body:
## Summary

Guard lazy reply-module loaders against `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` caused by stale
dist chunk hashes after an in-place `npm install -g` upgrade or rollback.
Previously, a running gateway process would silently drop all inbound messages
because the lazy `import()` resolved to chunk names that no longer existed on
disk. Now `isDistRotationError` detects the stale-dist condition, the guard
emits an operator-visible warning pointing to `systemctl restart`, and the
error propagates so the failure is no longer silent.

## Root Cause

After `npm install -g openclaw@<version>`, the `dist/` tree gets new chunk
hashes (e.g. `cleanup-DlVQZQex.js` → `cleanup-DbGY5-v-.js`). The running
gateway process still holds the old Node.js module cache, so a lazy dynamic
`import()` for a module that transitively references the old hash hits
`ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`. The failure surfaces only when an inbound message
arrives and triggers the lazy load — health checks and channel connections
look fine, so the operator sees a healthy gateway silently dropping messages.

The fix adds a narrow detection function (`isDistRotationError`) in the shared
`lazy-promise` module and wraps the seven lazy-load call sites in
`get-reply.ts` with a `guardedLoad` helper that clears the stale cache and logs
the restart instruction.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior
`isDistRotationError` discriminates `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` / `MODULE_NOT_FOUND`
errors whose message references `openclaw/dist/` (the tell for a hashed chunk
stale after update/rollback). `guardedLoad` catches that condition, clears the
stale loader promise so the next call can attempt a fresh import, emits an
operator-visible `error` + `warn` log pair, and re-throws so the inbound
delivery pipeline can surface the failure.

### environment
- OS: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64 (WSL2)
- Runtime: Node.js v22.22.0
- Setup: openclaw workspace at /home/0668000989/pr/openclaw, tsx 4.x

### steps
1. Write `scripts/repro-92241.ts` that imports `isDistRotationError` and
   `createLazyPromiseLoader` from production source
2. Construct synthetic `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` errors with `openclaw/dist/` in
   the message (positive) and without (negative)
3. Construct a mock `LazyPromiseLoader` that throws the dist-rotation error on
   load, pass it through a local `guardedLoad` mirroring `get-reply.ts:118`
4. Verify: (a) positive cases return true, (b) negative cases return false,
   (c) guardedLoad clears cache + logs warning + re-throws for dist rotation,
   (d) guardedLoad passes non-rotation errors through without clearing

### observedResult

**Reproduction script output:**
```
=== isDistRotationError: positive cases ===
  ✓ Linux ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND with openclaw/dist path → true
  ✓ Windows MODULE_NOT_FOUND with openclaw\dist path → true
  ✓ Hashed chunk ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND in dist → true

=== isDistRotationError: negative cases ===
  ✓ ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND outside openclaw → false
  ✓ ENOENT error → false
  ✓ Error without code → false
  ✓ null → false
  ✓ undefined → false
  ✓ string → false

=== guardedLoad: behavior verification ===
  ✓ guardedLoad: normal load returns value
  ✓ guardedLoad: normal load dedupes (single call)

--- dist rotation guard triggers (expected error output below) ---
[ERROR] auto-reply/reply-loader: bundled module changed under running gateway
after update/rollback — restart required (lazy module "stale-module" failed:
Cannot find module '/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/chunks/stale-hash.js')
[WARN] auto-reply/reply-loader: run "systemctl --user restart
openclaw-gateway.service" (or equivalent) to reload dist modules
  ✓ guardedLoad: dist rotation error re-thrown
  ✓ guardedLoad: dist rotation clears stale cache
  ✓ guardedLoad: dist rotation propagated to caller
--- non-rotation error (expected pass-through, no clear) ---
  ✓ guardedLoad: non-rotation error does NOT clear cache
  ✓ guardedLoad: non-rotation error still re-thrown

=== Results: 16 passed, 0 failed ===
```

**Test suite:**
```
✓ src/shared/lazy-promise.test.ts (11 tests) 11 passed
   (includes 6 new isDistRotationError cases:
    ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND + dist path → true,
    MODULE_NOT_FOUND + Windows dist path → true,
    outside dist → false, unrelated code → false,
    no code → false, null/non-object → false)
```

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `pnpm test -- --run src/shared/lazy-promise.test.ts` passes (11 tests, including 6 new `isDistRotationError` cases)
- [x] `tsx scripts/repro-92241.ts` passes (16 assertions, 0 failed)
- [ ] Change is minimal: 3 files, no new dependencies

---

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
