target.title: [codex] fix gateway hot-mode restart reloads
target.body:
## Summary

Closes the silent-staleness hole in `gateway.reload.mode: "hot"` without changing the shipped default contract. Hot mode keeps warn-and-keep for routine restart-required config edits; two scoped behaviors layer on top:

- **`gateway.reload.autoRestartOnRequired: true`** (new optional key, default `false`): opt-in that delegates restart-required edits to the gateway's existing idle-aware restart path, matching `mode: "hybrid"` for the restart-required branch while keeping hot-safe edits hot.
- **Security-critical carve-out** (always on): restart reasons under `gateway.auth.*` and `secrets.*` schedule a restart even in default hot mode. Without this, a `gateway.auth.token` rotation or `secrets.providers.*` change on disk sits behind warn-and-keep while the runtime keeps honoring the compromised credential.

An earlier draft of this PR made hot mode schedule restarts unconditionally; review correctly flagged that as a semantic change to the shipped `hot` contract, and the current shape supersedes it. Existing `mode: "hot"` deployments are behavior-stable on upgrade for routine edits.

## Details

- `src/gateway/config-reload.ts`: restart-required plans in hot mode now branch three ways — warn-and-keep (default), `hot mode scheduling restart per gateway.reload.autoRestartOnRequired=true` (opt-in), and `hot mode scheduling restart for security-critical change (...)` (always). The existing idle-aware restart machinery (`queueRestart`) remains the only restart path; this PR adds no new restart mechanism.
- `SECURITY_CRITICAL_RESTART_PREFIXES = ["gateway.auth", "secrets"]` — the `secrets` prefix is top-level, fixing the review-found P1 where an earlier `gateway.secrets` prefix could never match real restart reasons; `src/gateway/config-reload-plan.ts` now marks `secrets.providers.*` restart-required so the carve-out has a live trigger.
- `gateway.reload.autoRestartOnRequired` added to `src/config/types.gateway.ts`, `zod-schema.ts` (strict validation), `schema.help.ts`, `schema.labels.ts`.
- Regression tests in `src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts` pin each branch: warn-and-keep preserved on non-security edits; opt-in queues a supervised restart; security-critical auth/secrets changes restart regardless of the opt-in; plus the doctor-style model-route repair still hot-reloads when no restart-only path is present.
- Docs updated: `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, `docs/gateway/configuration.md`, `docs/gateway/index.md` describe the default, the opt-in, and the carve-out.

**Open maintainer decision:** this adds one config key on a compatibility-sensitive surface. The default split (warn-and-keep stays default; auth/secrets always restart) is the proposed contract — if a different split is preferred (e.g. opt-in defaulting `true` for fresh installs only), say the word and I will adjust.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: in hot reload mode, a config write touching any restart-required key was dropped with a single `log.warn` while the gateway kept serving from the stale in-memory snapshot. Observed in production as a 4-hour silent outage where the agent kept resolving the legacy `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` model id after `openclaw doctor --fix` had migrated the on-disk config to `openai/gpt-5.5`; group messages silently dropped, admin DMs returned a canned `[agent failed]` reply. With this patch, security-critical edits (auth/secrets) self-heal via restart by default, and operators can opt all restart-required edits into the same path.

- **Real environment tested**: isolated sandbox gateway on macOS (loopback port 28789), dist built from this branch; plus the original production incident capture on a launchd-supervised deployed gateway (`gateway-host`) documenting the pre-fix failure mode. Sandbox scenarios were captured at commit `d023afb9c4` (the commit introducing the opt-in + carve-out design); the secrets-prefix P1 fix and rebase landed after and are pinned by regression tests.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: for each scenario, start a fresh sandbox gateway from the branch dist, wait for the config watcher to settle, write one config edit, observe the reload log and process state.
  ```
  Scenario A: mode "hot" (defaults), edit gateway.bind "loopback" -> "lan"
  Scenario B: mode "hot" + autoRestartOnRequired true, same edit
  Scenario C: mode "hot" (defaults), rotate gateway.auth.token
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  A: [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (gateway.bind)
     process state: RUNNING (pid=8610, uptime 00:14)        # warn-and-keep preserved
  B: [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode scheduling restart per gateway.reload.autoRestartOnRequired=true (gateway.bind)
     [gateway] received SIGUSR1; restarting
     [shutdown] started: gateway restarting
     process state: RUNNING (pid=6706, uptime 00:15)        # restarted clean
  C: [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode scheduling restart for security-critical change (gateway.auth.token)
     [gateway] received SIGUSR1; restarting
     process state: RUNNING (pid=9261, uptime 00:14)        # credential rotation propagated
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: scenario A proves existing hot-mode deployments keep the shipped warn-and-keep contract with no restart. Scenario B proves the opt-in routes routine restart-required edits through the existing SIGUSR1 + clean-shutdown restart path. Scenario C proves auth rotations propagate by default, closing the credential-rotation-bypass gap. The production failure mode (`hot mode ignoring` on `auth.profiles.*` for 4 hours) is eliminated for security-critical keys and operator-electable for everything else.

- **What was not tested**: launchd auto-respawn end-to-end on hosts using `KeepAlive: { SuccessfulExit: false }` (see operator note below — the clean-exit hand-off needs a kickstart watcher on such hosts; not a regression, the pre-fix path also required manual intervention). The sandbox ran unmanaged (in-process restart mode); the managed-install launchd restart path is covered by existing integration tests. Scenario captures predate the final rebase; the post-rebase head is pinned by 360 passing gateway-config tests including the three branch-behavior regressions.

## Operator note: launchd `SuccessfulExit=false` hosts

Hosts that set `KeepAlive: { Crashed=true, SuccessfulExit=false }` (to dodge an unrelated respawn cascade) will not auto-respawn after the restart hand-off's clean exit. The deployment fork pairs this with a 30s interval watchdog (`launchctl print` state AND port-binding check, then `launchctl kickstart -k`) — documented here so the gap flagged in review is paired with its operational answer. Hosts on default launchd `KeepAlive` semantics respawn normally and need nothing.

target.title: fix(reply): also drop tool-error progress payloads under messages.suppressToolErrors
target.body:
## Summary

`messages.suppressToolErrors` is an existing user-facing config knob that hides tool-error noise from chat (landed in #16620 / #81561). Today it only drops the warning TEXT inside `src/auto-reply/reply/payloads.ts`. The error tool-result payload is still delivered as channel progress through the `onToolResult` path in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` unless `sourceReplyDeliveryMode === "message_tool_only"`. Operators who set `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` therefore still see tool-error noise as progress payloads, contradicting the documented behavior of the knob.

## Change

Add a config-gated early-return inside `onToolResult` in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` that drops the visible progress delivery when `payload.isError === true` and `replyConfig.messages?.suppressToolErrors === true`, regardless of `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`. Per review feedback, the gate is positioned before the optional source-delivery callback, so a suppressed error payload never reaches progress delivery or the source callback. A focused regression test in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` exercises the suppressed-error path through `dispatchReplyFromConfig` and asserts neither `onToolResult` nor `sendToolResult` is called while the final reply still ships.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: when `messages.suppressToolErrors: true`, tool-result payloads carrying `isError: true` must not be delivered as channel progress. Non-error tool results and the final reply must continue to flow normally. When `suppressToolErrors` is unset or false, tool-error progress must still deliver (no opt-in, no behavior change).

Real environment tested: live deployed openclaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 71842, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 includes this commit; deployed bundle `dist/dispatch-D9SNCck_.js` contains the new gate at line 1458).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: copied `/tmp/proof-toolerr-amittell.mjs` to rh-bot.lan and ran `node /tmp/proof-toolerr-amittell.mjs` against the deployed bundle. The script imports `dispatchReplyFromConfig` from the deployed `dist/dispatch-D9SNCck_.js` and `finalizeInboundContext` from the deployed `dist/inbound-context-CbkangX1.js`, builds a minimal telegram-direct ctx, a dispatcher with spy counters on `sendToolResult` / `sendFinalReply`, and a `replyResolver` that fires `onToolResult` with the toggled `isError` flag before returning a final payload. Three scenarios were exercised:

  A. `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` + `isError: true`
  B. `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` + `isError: false`
  C. `messages.suppressToolErrors: false` + `isError: true`

`agents.defaults.verboseDefault: "on"` was set so the verbose-progress gate did not mask the assertion.

Evidence after fix: deployed bundle gate verified at `/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/dispatch-D9SNCck_.js:1458` as `if (payload.isError === true && replyConfig.messages?.suppressToolErrors === true) return;`. Script output:

```
--- scenario A: suppressToolErrors=true, isError=true (gate should drop tool progress)
    calls: {"sendToolResult":0,"sendBlockReply":0,"sendFinalReply":1}
PASS A sendToolResult NOT called (tool-error progress dropped)
PASS A sendFinalReply called once (final still delivered)
--- scenario B: suppressToolErrors=true, isError=false (preserve normal delivery)
    calls: {"sendToolResult":1,"sendBlockReply":0,"sendFinalReply":1}
PASS B sendToolResult called >=1 (non-error tool result delivered normally)
PASS B sendFinalReply called once
--- scenario C: suppressToolErrors=false, isError=true (preserve normal delivery, no opt-in)
    calls: {"sendToolResult":1,"sendBlockReply":0,"sendFinalReply":1}
PASS C sendToolResult called >=1 (no opt-in, tool error still delivered)
PASS C sendFinalReply called once
OVERALL: PASS
```

Observed result after fix: with `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` and `isError: true`, the deployed bundle dropped the tool-result progress delivery (`sendToolResult` count was 0) while the final reply still shipped (`sendFinalReply` count was 1). With either `suppressToolErrors: true` + non-error payload or `suppressToolErrors: false` + error payload, tool-result delivery was preserved (`sendToolResult` count was 1). Existing behavior is preserved outside the explicit opt-in.

What was not tested: an end-to-end Telegram round-trip through the live channel adapter and a paired check of channels that route through `message_tool_only` source-delivery (which the existing pre-fix path already covered). The change is purely a guard added to the dispatch-site progress path; channel adapter behavior is unchanged.

commands.review_summary:
The PR makes local gateway status and probe commands return structured missing-credential failures before opening a WebSocket, while preserving explicit URL overrides and cached paired-device authentication.

PR surface: Source +182, Tests +180. Total +362 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR supplies exact commands and built-runtime JSON output demonstrating both the local missing-auth short-circuit and the explicit URL connection control.

target.title: fix(gateway): fail fast on missing local probe auth
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a fail-fast in `openclaw gateway status` for the local loopback case: when `gateway.auth.mode` requires credentials but neither the persisted token nor an interactive credential can be resolved, the status command synthesises a failed probe with a clear `failureReason` instead of attempting an anonymous WebSocket connection that surfaces as a misleading `Connect: failed`.

Explicit `--url`/`--rpc-url` targets are intentionally exempt: a user pointing at a remote, forwarded, or open gateway should not be blocked by missing local-config credentials.

## Details

- `resolveAuthForTarget` resolves through the probe-surface helper. When the result has neither token nor password AND the target is the known local loopback AND `gateway.auth.mode` requires credentials, it falls back to the interactive-surface helper and propagates the failure reason.
- The fail-fast is scoped to `surface === "local" && target.kind === "localLoopback"`. Targets with `kind === "explicit"` (i.e. `--url`/`--rpc-url`) skip the check.
- Tests cover both the local fail-fast path and the explicit-target carve-out, and isolate ambient `gateway.auth.token` env in the probe-auth test to avoid cross-test contamination.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: before this patch, `openclaw gateway status` against a local config with `gateway.auth.mode: "token"` but an unresolvable token (e.g. `secrets.token.env` points at a non-existent env var) would still try an anonymous WebSocket connection and surface `Connect: failed` to the operator. The operator-actionable diagnosis ("missing required local auth") was buried under a misleading network error. After this patch, the probe is short-circuited with a `failureReason` that names the auth shape.

- **Real environment tested**: deployed `openclaw` runtime built from this PR head on a local laptop checkout at `/tmp/wt-68280` (rebased onto `upstream/main` at `8e1b0e61b0`), run against an isolated test config at `/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json` so the live mac-mini gateway and its real auth are not touched. The test config sets `gateway.auth.mode: "token"` and `gateway.secrets.token.env` to an env var that is explicitly unset in the shell.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  unset OPENCLAW_PROBE_NONEXISTENT_TEST_VAR
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json node dist/index.js gateway status --json | jq '.rpc | {ok, error, kind}'
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/probe-test-68280/openclaw.json node dist/index.js gateway status --url ws://127.0.0.1:18999 --json | jq '{rpc: .rpc | {ok, error, kind}, probeUrl: .gateway.probeUrl}'
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Captured live from the patched `node dist/index.js` runtime built from this PR head. The first scenario is the local-loopback fail-fast; the second is the explicit-`--url` carve-out.

  ```
  $ node dist/index.js gateway status --json | jq '.rpc | {ok, error, kind}'
  {
    "ok": false,
    "error": "Missing gateway auth token.",
    "kind": null
  }

  $ node dist/index.js gateway status --url ws://127.0.0.1:18999 --json | jq '{rpc: .rpc | {ok, error, kind}, probeUrl: .gateway.probeUrl}'
  {
    "rpc": {
      "ok": false,
      "error": "connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:18999",
      "kind": "connect"
    },
    "probeUrl": "ws://127.0.0.1:18999"
  }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: in the local-loopback scenario, `rpc.kind` is `null` (no probe attempt was made) and `rpc.error` names the actionable auth diagnostic instead of a network error. In the explicit-URL scenario, `rpc.kind` is `"connect"` (the probe DID run) and the error is a real network-level `ECONNREFUSED` against the URL the operator supplied. The design boundary holds: missing local SecretRef diagnostics do not leak into the explicit-URL probe contract.

- **What was not tested**: the explicit-URL daemon `--rpc-url` flag was not exercised end-to-end in this proof. The carve-out is structural (`target.kind !== "explicit"` is the only gate) and is covered by the regression tests added in `c6c648a9cd` and `8e1b0e61b0`.

target.title: fix(agents): forward turn-source for embedded plugin approvals (supersedes #84205)
target.body:
## Summary

Forwards turn-source routing fields through the embedded plugin `before_tool_call` approval chain so plugin approvals can be delivered back to the originating chat (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.). Without this, embedded-runner plugin approvals reach `requestPluginToolApproval` without a delivery route and resolve as `decision: null` (no-approval-route), blocking the tool while the model often hallucinates a fake `/approve` instruction and a synthesized success.

Supersedes #84205 (stale since 2026-05-19, blocked from updates because the PR branch is on `jzakirov/openclaw-official`). This PR carries the same intent as #84205, applied to the current `agent-tools.*` file layout (the upstream rename of `pi-tools.*` -> `agent-tools.*` happened after #84205 was opened), and extends it with two additional changes that turned up while validating the fix on a production deployment fork.

After ClawSweeper review the workshop fallback was tightened from `unavailableBehavior: "allow"` to the default fail-closed contract so mutating skill_workshop actions (`apply`, `reject`, `quarantine`) cannot bypass the operator-intended pending-approval default when no approval route is available.

## Details

Two commits on the current head (the original four-commit stack was squashed during the 2026-06-11 rebase; their content is unchanged and described below):

1. `ae2aa9c399` "forward turn-source for embedded plugin approvals" - the squashed fix. Extends the embedded-mode hook context with `turnSource*` fields and threads them through `requestPluginToolApproval` from every embedded entry point (codex app-server dynamic-tools and run-attempt, CLI runner, embedded-agent-runner, openclaw-tools, gateway tools-invoke-shared) and through the `createOpenClawCodingTools` factory that produces the HookContext consumed by every downstream plugin approval request. Also removes the `unavailableBehavior: "allow"` carve-out per ClawSweeper P1 review - the runtime always fails closed on no-route; the model sees an immediate `Plugin approval unavailable` block instead of a synthesized ALLOW_ONCE.
2. `f5795713c2` "test(gateway): prove plugin-approval turn-source round trip at the invoke boundary" - the isolated fixture round-trip offered in review as the non-mutating alternative to a live Telegram approval round-trip (see below).

## Fixture round-trip proof (review ask)

`src/gateway/tools-invoke-shared.turn-source-approval.test.ts` drives the real `invokeGatewayTool` -> `before_tool_call` -> `plugin.approval.request` chain with only the gateway RPC transport stubbed:

- A plugin tool gated by a trusted-tool approval policy is invoked with `messageChannel: "telegram"`, `agentTo: "tg:12345"`, `accountId: "acct-1"`, `agentThreadId: "77"`; the emitted `plugin.approval.request` payload carries all four `turnSource*` fields verbatim, and the tool executes after ALLOW_ONCE.
- The captured payload is fed through the real `createApprovalNativeRouteReporter` with approver-DM-only delivery and no plan-side origin target: the coordinator resolves the origin notice target to exactly `{channel: "telegram", to: "tg:12345", accountId: "acct-1", threadId: "77"}` - the turn-source binding is the only possible source.
- Negative path: `decision: null` (no approval route) blocks fail-closed (`403`, `Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)`), and with no turn-source binding the coordinator resolves no origin target and stays silent instead of guessing a chat.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: plugin `before_tool_call` requireApproval requests originating from embedded-runner turns (CLI, codex, embedded-agent) had no `turnSource*` routing fields, so the gateway resolved them as `decision: null` (no-approval-route) and blocked the tool. The same fields were already wired for bash/exec approvals, so plugin approvals were the asymmetric gap. Observed in production on the deployment fork as Telegram-originated `skill_workshop apply` actions that never reached a deliverable approval prompt.

- **Real environment tested**: deployed `openclaw` runtime built from this PR head on a local laptop checkout at `/tmp/turnsource-pr`, branch `fix/embedded-plugin-approval-turn-source-superset` rebased on `upstream/main` at `e59e65be67`. Build commit `ef5bb8d408`. Production gateways on `gateway-host` (`user@gateway-host`) are running the same commits cherry-picked into the deployment fork (`upgrade-v2026.6.2` at `3ef6591a4e`) and were verified at deploy time.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  cd /tmp/turnsource-pr
  pnpm build
  grep -c "turnSourceChannel" dist/*.js
  grep -F "Plugin approval unavailable" dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-*.js
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts -t "skill_workshop"
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Captured live from the patched `dist/` bundle built from this PR head.

  ```
  $ git log --oneline -4
  ef5bb8d408 fix(skills): block skill_workshop lifecycle approvals when no route is available
  5734dad75e wip(agents): thread turn-source through createOpenClawCodingTools factory
  713649e11e wip(agents): thread turn-source through embedded plugin approval contexts
  f5ef9d2b6f Allow skill workshop lifecycle without approval route

  $ grep -c "turnSourceChannel\|turnSourceTo\|turnSourceAccountId\|turnSourceThreadId" dist/*.js | grep -v ":0" | head -10
  dist/agent-command-CIibMkGi.js:4
  dist/agent-CnqqLDfu.js:12
  dist/agent-delivery-CAkfTZyN.js:9
  dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-C4q8C-bA.js:4
  dist/agent-tools-BvNXDsuT.js:4
  dist/approval-delivery-helpers-BCbYC_RR.js:1
  dist/approval-native-brfCkLin.js:4
  dist/approval-native-BI79BlFD.js:6
  dist/approval-native-route-coordinator-C4wbDCvi.js:5

  $ grep -F "Plugin approval unavailable" dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-*.js
  dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-C4q8C-bA.js:				reason: "Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)",

  $ grep -A6 "function resolveSkillWorkshopToolApproval" dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-*.js
  function resolveSkillWorkshopToolApproval(params) {
    if (params.toolName !== "skill_workshop") return;
    const action = readLifecycleAction(params.toolParams);
    if (!action) return;
    if (resolveSkillWorkshopConfig(params.config).approvalPolicy === "auto") return;

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts -t "skill_workshop"
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  2 passed | 22 skipped (24)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: (1) turnSource* fields propagate into the compiled embedded-runner bundle across 10+ files; (2) `resolveSkillWorkshopToolApproval` in the compiled bundle no longer emits `unavailableBehavior: "allow"`, so a no-route approval for `skill_workshop apply/reject/quarantine` falls through to the default fail-closed branch (`blocked: true, deniedReason: "plugin-approval", reason: "Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)"`); (3) the regression test on the post-fix branch passes both the original `reasoning` round-trip and the new `blocks skill_workshop lifecycle approvals when no route is available` assertion.

- **What was not tested**: a live Telegram round-trip of a `skill_workshop apply` against the running `gateway-host` was not driven to keep production traffic undisturbed. The fixture round-trip test on the current head (`f5795713c2`) now covers the same chain at the invoke boundary with only the RPC transport stubbed; the compiled `dist/` bundle confirms the runtime code matches the source, and the fail-closed change is conservative (a stricter contract than the previous behavior), so the deployment risk is bounded to "skill_workshop runs without a live approval route now fail-fast instead of synthesizing ALLOW_ONCE".

> Note: SHAs in the evidence block above (`ef5bb8d408` etc.) are the pre-squash commits whose content now lives in `ae2aa9c399`; the dist grep and test output were captured from that identical content.

target.title: fix(exec-approvals): skip socket in YOLO mode regardless of token presence
target.body:
## Summary

When `exec-approvals.json` has a socket token (e.g. after `ensureExecApprovals()` was called by an external approvals write like `openclaw approvals set --node`), the fast-path check in `resolveExecApprovals()` failed its third condition (`!file.socket?.token?.trim()`), forcing the socket code path even in YOLO mode (`security=full, ask=off`).

Since the approvals Unix socket is never created or listened on by the node host process, this caused all `system.run` invocations on the node to return `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED: approval required`.

## Fix

Remove the token condition from the fast-path guard. In YOLO mode no approval is ever requested, so the token (and the socket it references) is irrelevant. The fast path is still guarded by `security === "full" && ask === "off"`.

```diff
-    if (
-      resolved.agent.security === "full" &&
-      resolved.agent.ask === "off" &&
-      !file.socket?.token?.trim()
-    ) {
+    if (resolved.agent.security === "full" && resolved.agent.ask === "off") {
       return resolved;
     }
```

## Files changed

- `src/infra/exec-approvals.ts`: 1 insertion, 5 deletions

## Related

Fixes #92330

## Real behavior proof

**Setup:**
- Environment: Kali Linux node (headless, systemd user service), connected as OpenClaw child node
- Policy: YOLO mode (`security=full, ask=off`) configured via `openclaw approvals set`
- OpenClaw version: 2026.6.x

**Before fix (observed failure):**
1. Node was stuck returning `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED: approval required` for all `system.run` invocations despite YOLO policy
2. Root cause traced to `exec-approvals.json` containing a `socket.token` field persisted from `ensureExecApprovals()`
3. The fast-path guard `!file.socket?.token?.trim()` was permanently `false`, forcing the socket code path
4. Since the approvals Unix socket was never created on the node host, the socket path returned denial for every request

**After fix (verified):**
1. Applied the patch — removed `!file.socket?.token?.trim()` from the fast-path condition
2. Restarted node — exec calls immediately returned through the fast path
3. `system.run` invocations work correctly regardless of whether `exec-approvals.json` contains a socket token

**Code-level verification:**
- The fast path remains guarded by `security === "full" && ask === "off"` — no weakening of the approval policy
- In YOLO mode, the socket path is never consulted, so removing the token check cannot introduce a new denial path
- The removed condition was the ONLY barrier between the fast-path return and the socket code path — if the socket token is present but the socket isn't listening, the only possible outcome was denial

**Not tested:**
- Interactive approval flow with an active Unix socket (unaffected — the fast path only triggers in YOLO mode)
- Multi-node deployments with mixed approval policies

**CI:** Full test suite running on this PR.

target.title: fix(gateway): mirror hidden channel assistant commentary
target.body:
## Problem

Channel-originated sessions can subscribe to session-message events without the Control UI being visible. That hidden-session path already forwards tool events, but assistant commentary frames were not mirrored to those session-message subscribers.

ClickClack depends on those hidden session-message events to build its preamble. The result was asymmetric live behavior:

- tool calls flowed into ClickClack
- assistant commentary did not
- the final answer still arrived, so the failure looked like a missing preamble rather than a broken run

This is separate from #92092. That PR fixed claude-cli narration being dropped before gateway delivery. This PR fixes the gateway fanout path for hidden/channel sessions after assistant commentary already exists as an agent event.

## Root cause

`createAgentEventHandler()` had two distinct fanout behaviors:

1. Control-UI-visible runs: assistant events are sent through the normal visible UI agent-event path.
2. Non-Control-UI-visible runs: selected session-message subscribers get hidden live chat/tool updates, but assistant commentary events fall through and are not sent to those exact subscribers.

The hidden path was therefore complete for tool activity but incomplete for assistant commentary.

## Fix

Add a narrow hidden-session mirror path for assistant commentary events:

- only runs when `isControlUiVisible` is false
- only sends to exact session-message subscribers for the resolved session key
- only forwards assistant events that are commentary or streaming assistant deltas
- explicitly excludes `final_answer` so final replies are not double-posted
- preserves abort suppression
- uses the existing hidden `sendAgentPayload(..., { controlUiVisible: false, dropIfSlow: true })` route

## Contract documentation

This PR also adds `docs/channels/agent-event-io-contract.md`, a provider-input and channel-output contract for this boundary. It documents the normalized `assistant`, `tool`, `item`, and `lifecycle` expectations, explicitly covers delta-only assistant frames, hidden channel-session mirroring, final-answer exclusion, and channel projection into preamble/tool activity rows.

The contract is included here because the ClickClack failure exposed an undocumented seam: provider/runtime events existed, but hidden channel subscribers did not receive the assistant-commentary subset. Future provider or channel work should not have to rediscover that mapping.

## Tests

Focused gateway regression coverage in `src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts`:

- commentary with `phase: "commentary"` is mirrored to exact hidden session subscribers
- assistant streaming delta without an explicit phase is mirrored
- assistant text without a delta is not mirrored
- `phase: "final_answer"` is not mirrored
- hidden commentary is not globally broadcast
- hidden commentary is not sent via `nodeSendToSession`
- other session subscribers do not receive the event
- aborted hidden runs do not mirror commentary

Validation run:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts --maxWorkers=1

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       87 passed (87)
```

Additional checks:

```text
$ pnpm tsgo:core
# clean

$ pnpm tsgo:test:src
# clean

$ git diff --check
# clean

$ pnpm docs:check-mdx
# Docs MDX check passed

$ pnpm lint:docs
# markdownlint passed
```

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: hidden/channel session subscribers received tool events but not assistant commentary events, preventing ClickClack from building commentary preambles for channel-originated user turns.

- Real environment tested: OpenClaw gateway built from this patch and restarted on `2026-06-11T15:31Z`; ClickClack bridge subscribed to the real channel session `agent:chisel:clickclack:channel:channel:chn_01ktq4zg9bga4dqrycqz67jts6`; real ClickClack `#test` and `#general` turns driven by ragesaq through the deployed bridge.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  1. Built the OpenClaw gateway from branch `fix/channel-hidden-commentary`.
  2. Restarted `openclaw-gateway.service` and `clickclack-bridge.service`.
  3. Drove channel-originated ClickClack turns from ragesaq in `#test` and `#general`.
  4. Confirmed ClickClack persisted `agent_commentary` rows alongside `agent_tool` rows after deploy.
  5. Captured before/after ClickClack screenshots from the real user-facing channel UI.

- Evidence after fix:

  Before this patch, ragesaq's channel-originated ClickClack turn reached the final answer, but no commentary/tool preamble rendered for the long-running turn:

  ![before: final answer without commentary preamble](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ragesaq/openclaw/proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence/.proof/channel-hidden-commentary/before-real-user-channel-proof.png)

  After this patch, ragesaq's channel-originated ClickClack turn renders an expanded preamble with assistant commentary interleaved with tool-call rows:

  ![after: expanded preamble with commentary and tool calls](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ragesaq/openclaw/proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence/.proof/channel-hidden-commentary/after-real-user-channel-proof.png)

  ClickClack also persisted real `agent_commentary` rows in the channel alongside `agent_tool` rows. Example rows from the ClickClack DB after deploy:

```text
2026-06-11T15:55:29.371130227Z  agent_commentary  Pushed (`6055c05`). Posting the summary to the channel.
2026-06-11T15:55:21.602563769Z  agent_tool        **exec**
2026-06-11T15:55:41.92467386Z   agent_tool        **message**
```

  Additional user-visible proof screenshots are committed on fork branch `proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence`:

  - expanded preamble, commentary interleaved with tool-call rows: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ragesaq/openclaw/proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence/.proof/channel-hidden-commentary/preamble-expanded-commentary-tools.png
  - collapsed preamble with final answer still visible: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ragesaq/openclaw/proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence/.proof/channel-hidden-commentary/preamble-collapsed-final-visible.png
  - proof note: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ragesaq/openclaw/proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence/.proof/channel-hidden-commentary/real-behavior-proof.md

- Observed result after fix: ClickClack receives hidden assistant commentary frames for the exact channel session, persists them as durable `agent_commentary` messages, and renders a user-visible preamble containing commentary plus tool-call rows while the final answer remains visible.

- What was not tested: upstream-maintainer-hosted deployment. This PR also does not change or test ClickClack rendering internals; it fixes the OpenClaw gateway fanout path that ClickClack already consumes.

## Risk checklist

- **Behavior change scope:** hidden/channel session-message subscribers now receive assistant commentary events they were already subscribed to but missing. Control-UI-visible behavior is untouched.
- **Final-answer duplication:** explicitly guarded by excluding `phase === "final_answer"`.
- **Session isolation:** uses the existing exact session-message subscriber registry and test asserts only the selected session receives events.
- **Abort behavior:** preserved and covered by regression test.
- **Persistence/schema:** no schema or storage changes.
- **Backpressure:** uses the existing hidden subscriber `dropIfSlow: true` behavior.

## Not in scope

- ClickClack rendering implementation. This PR fixes the OpenClaw gateway event fanout path only.
- #92092's claude-cli parser lane fix. That bug was upstream of this path and has already merged.

## AI assistance

AI-assisted. Authored under ragesaq's OpenClaw contributor identity with Forge and Chisel assistance. ragesaq supplied the real before/after ClickClack screenshots and directed the proof update; Chisel prepared this PR body/proof revision with `openai/gpt-5.5`.

target.title: fix(utils): make parseBooleanValue case-insensitive for custom lists and guard against malformed inputs
target.body:
Summary

   - Problem: parseBooleanValue was case-sensitive when using custom truthy/falsy lists and would throw a TypeError if those
     options were provided as non-arrays (e.g., from untyped JSON or JS plugins).
   - Why it matters: Prevents runtime crashes when processing malformed or untyped configuration data and makes custom boolean
     parsing more robust and user-friendly.
   - What changed: 
       - Added Array.isArray guards before mapping custom truthy/falsy lists.
       - Implemented case-insensitivity for custom truthy/falsy overrides.
       - Added comprehensive regression tests.
   - What did NOT change: The core logic for default truthy/falsy values remains unchanged.

  Change Type (select all)

   - [x] Bug fix
   - [ ] Feature
   - [ ] Refactor required for the fix
   - [ ] Docs
   - [x] Security hardening
   - [ ] Chore/infra

  Scope (select all touched areas)

   - [ ] Gateway / orchestration
   - [ ] Skills / tool execution
   - [ ] Auth / tokens
   - [ ] Memory / storage
   - [ ] Integrations
   - [x] API / contracts
   - [x] UI / DX
   - [ ] CI/CD / infra

  Linked Issue/PR

   - Related #59535 (previous attempt)
   - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

  Root Cause (if applicable)

   - Root cause: The implementation unconditionally called .map() on options.truthy and options.falsy, assuming they were always
     arrays. It also missed case normalization for these custom strings.
   - Missing detection / guardrail: Lack of type validation for runtime-injected options.
   - Contributing context: Identified during Codex review of a previous merge.

  Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

   - Coverage level that should have caught this:
     - [x] Unit test
     - [ ] Seam / integration test
     - [ ] End-to-end test
     - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
   - Target test or file: src/utils/utils-misc.test.ts
   - Scenario the test should lock in:
       - Passing non-array values to truthy/falsy options (should return undefined instead of crashing).
       - Passing mixed-case custom strings (should match regardless of case).
       - Passing non-string entries in custom lists.
   - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises the utility function with the problematic input shapes.

  User-visible / Behavior Changes

   - Custom boolean parsing via parseBooleanValue is now case-insensitive for custom lists.
   - Malformed configuration for custom truthy/falsy lists will no longer crash the process.

  Diagram (if applicable)

   1 Before:
   2 parseBooleanValue("yes", { truthy: "YES" }) -> TypeError: truthy.map is not a function
   3
   4 After:
   5 parseBooleanValue("yes", { truthy: "YES" }) -> undefined (graceful fail)
   6 parseBooleanValue("yes", { truthy: ["YES"] }) -> true (case-insensitive match)

  Security Impact (required)

   - New permissions/capabilities? (No)
   - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
   - New/changed network calls? (No)
   - Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
   - Data access scope changed? (No)

  Repro + Verification

  Environment

   - OS: darwin (macOS)
   - Runtime: Node.js v23.11.0

  Steps

   1. Call parseBooleanValue("yes", { truthy: "YES" })
   2. Call parseBooleanValue("yes", { truthy: ["YES"] })

  Expected

   1. Returns undefined (or previous behavior of ignoring invalid option).
   2. Returns true.

  Actual

   1. Threw TypeError: truthy.map is not a function.
   2. Returned undefined (case-sensitive mismatch).

  Evidence

   - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Verified via pnpm exec vitest run src/utils/utils-misc.test.ts)

  Human Verification (required)

   - Verified scenarios: Malformed options (string, object), mixed-case custom matches, non-string entries in lists.
   - Edge cases checked: Empty strings, null values in lists, default behavior when no options are provided.
   - What you did not verify: Integration with every single plugin that might use this (verified at the utility level).

  Review Conversations

   - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
   - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

  Compatibility / Migration

   - Backward compatible? (Yes)
   - Config/env changes? (No)
   - Migration needed? (No)

  Risks and Mitigations

   - None: The fix adds guards and increases flexibility without breaking existing functionality.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(openai-embedding): preserve openai/ prefix for non-native base URLs
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve `openai/` model prefix for non-native OpenAI embedding base URLs
- When `agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider` is `openai` but `remote.baseUrl` points to a non-native OpenAI router (e.g. Requesty), OpenClaw was stripping the `openai/` prefix from the embedding model name, breaking memory search in two ways: non-native routers reject the unqualified model name, and existing memory index identity no longer matches, forcing an index rebuild
- Root cause: `normalizeOpenAiModel()` unconditionally strips the `openai/` prefix, but the resolved base URL determines whether the prefix should be preserved
- This PR adds `isNativeOpenAiBaseUrl()` to check the resolved base URL, restores the `openai/` prefix for non-native URLs, and normalizes the model name before the `maxInputTokens` capability lookup so token caps are not lost when the model is qualified
- Fixes #92124

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/embedding-provider.test.ts` — 10 passed (4 new: native URL strips prefix, non-native preserves prefix, non-native prefix in request body, maxInputTokens preserved for qualified model)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-model-normalize.test.ts` — 3 passed (existing prefix stripping behavior)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI embedding model prefix is stripped for non-native OpenAI-compatible routers (e.g. Requesty), causing request rejection and memory index identity mismatch
Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22, branch fix/issue-92124-embedding-prefix
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/embedding-provider.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```console
 RUN  v4.1.7

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
   Start at  12:17:51
   Duration  1.85s
```

Observed result after fix: All 10 tests pass, including 4 new tests covering native URL prefix stripping, non-native prefix preservation, non-native prefix in request body, and maxInputTokens lookup for qualified model names
What was not tested: Live Requesty endpoint — the fix is a pure classifier change (base URL comparison and model string manipulation) fully covered by unit tests; no external service dependency

target.title: fix(sessions): persist sender metadata in user turn transcript JSONL
target.body:
## Summary

- Thread `senderId`/`senderName`/`senderUsername` from the channel inbound context into the persisted user-turn transcript message
- Group chat session JSONL records now include `__openclaw` sender identity metadata, matching the metadata already present in direct (1:1) session records
- When no sender metadata is available (empty/null values), the `__openclaw` block is omitted — preserving existing behavior
- `senderE164` (phone number) is intentionally excluded from the persisted fields for privacy — maintainers can add it back if needed

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix / [ ] Feature / [ ] Refactor / [ ] Docs / [ ] Security / [ ] Chore

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / [ ] Skills / [ ] Auth / [ ] Memory / [ ] Integrations / [ ] API / [ ] UI/UX / [x] Sessions / [ ] CI

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Group chat user messages persisted to the session JSONL transcript were missing `__openclaw` sender identity metadata (`senderId`, `senderName`, etc.) that is present in direct (1:1) session records.

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, pnpm test + production function via `node --import tsx`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Called the actual production `appendUserTurnTranscriptMessage` function from source via `node --import tsx` with sender metadata fields, then read back the JSONL transcript to verify `__openclaw` envelope presence and field values.

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { appendUserTurnTranscriptMessage } from './src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.ts';
import fs from 'node:fs';
import os from 'node:os';
import path from 'node:path';

const dir = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'openclaw-proof-'));
const transcriptPath = path.join(dir, 'session.jsonl');

const result = await appendUserTurnTranscriptMessage({
  transcriptPath, sessionId: 'session-proof', sessionKey: 'proof', cwd: dir,
  input: {
    text: 'hello from group chat',
    senderId: '8489979671',
    senderName: 'Ram Shenoy',
    senderUsername: 'ram_s',
  },
  updateMode: 'none',
});

const rawLines = fs.readFileSync(transcriptPath, 'utf-8').trim().split('\n');
const msg = JSON.parse(rawLines.filter(l => l.trim()).slice(-1)[0]).message;
console.log('Has __openclaw:', '__openclaw' in msg);
console.log('__openclaw:', JSON.stringify(msg.__openclaw));
"
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
Has __openclaw: true
__openclaw: {"senderId":"8489979671","senderName":"Ram Shenoy","senderUsername":"ram_s"}
```

No-sender case (empty/null values) correctly omits `__openclaw`:

```text
No-sender case - has __openclaw: false
```

**Observed result after the fix:** Group chat user turn messages persisted via the production `appendUserTurnTranscriptMessage` now include `__openclaw` sender identity metadata in the JSONL output.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway session with real Telegram group chat message delivery.

## Root Cause

The shared `UserTurnInput` → `buildPersistedUserTurnMessage` path only persisted text, media, and provenance. Sender identity from the channel inbound context (`SenderId`, `SenderName`, `SenderUsername`) was carried into the agent runtime but not threaded into the user-turn transcript message written to the session JSONL.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target: `src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts`
- Two new test cases: one asserting `__openclaw` envelope with sender metadata, one asserting `__openclaw` is omitted when no sender metadata is provided
- Existing 25+ tests continue to pass

Ref #90531

target.title: fix(wechat): preserve existing accounts across hot reload
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve known live accounts when a channel hot-reload restarts without an explicit account id
- treat hot-reload stop/restart as a manager-owned handoff so existing accounts restart instead of getting stuck in a manual-stop state
- document the WeChat multi-account behavior and cover the hot-reload path with regression tests
- add a guard that ordinary caller-owned restarts still do not resurrect stale accounts
- harden the non-isolated agent model-catalog visibility coverage that timed out in refreshed CI

## Why
Adding a second WeChat account can temporarily surface only the new account in the plugin account listing. Before this change, the gateway could evict or fail to restart the already-running account during hot reload, leaving account A offline after account B logs in.

## Testing
- `pnpm install`
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts` (3 files, 64 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts` (1 file, 24 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` (Node 24.15.0; 1 file, 3 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.moonshot.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.policy.test.ts` (Node 24.15.0; 5 files, 15 tests passed)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: plugin hot reload for `openclaw-weixin` treats the pre-replace channel stop as a manager-owned handoff and restarts the active channel with known accounts included.

Real environment tested: clean temporary OpenClaw contributor worktree on Rohin's Mac mini at commit `82f88803b6`; Node v25.9.0; pnpm 11.2.2.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: the gateway hot-reload regression suite passed with 3 test files and 64 tests, including the plugin pre-replace rollback and deferred channel reload paths that restart with `{ includeKnownAccounts: true }`, plus the ordinary restart guard that proves stale known accounts are not resurrected outside the hot-reload safety-net path.

Observed result after fix: the hot-reload path calls `stopChannel("openclaw-weixin", undefined, { manual: false })` before plugin replacement, then `startChannel("openclaw-weixin", undefined, { includeKnownAccounts: true })` after replacement, so the manager-owned reload path does not mark the account as manually stopped before account preservation runs.

What was not tested: live WeChat login/account A+B provider session was not run from this host; redacted live WeChat proof remains the provider-level validation if maintainers require it.

## AI assisted
- OpenAI Codex

target.title: fix(usage): handle Daylight saving time (DST) hour boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

This fixes a Control UI Usage-page freeze when a long session spans a local Daylight saving time (DST) transition.

Today the hour-slicing code computes a local `:59:59.999` hour end from the current wall-clock time. In a repeated local hour at Daylight saving time (DST) fallback, that can resolve to an earlier instant and stop cursor progress.

This change switches Usage hour slicing and selected-hour filtering to monotonic next-hour-boundary stepping so aggregation always moves forward in the selected timezone.

## Why

This fixes cases where:

- Usage is rendered in local time
- a session spans a Daylight saving time (DST) fallback or spring-forward boundary
- the page builds hour buckets or applies a selected-hour filter

Without this change, the Control UI can wedge the browser main thread while rendering Usage data for long sessions.

## Changes

- replace the ambiguous local end-of-hour calculation with `getNextHourStart()` plus bounded next-hour stepping in `ui/src/ui/views/usage-metrics.ts`
- convert session hour slicing to half-open intervals `[cursor, nextBoundary)` so exact boundaries do not create zero-duration buckets
- route the selected-hour filter in `ui/src/ui/views/usage.ts` through the same DST-safe helper instead of maintaining a duplicate loop
- add focused node-side regression coverage for Daylight saving time (DST) fallback, Daylight saving time (DST) spring-forward, peak-error aggregation, and full Usage render with an hour filter

## Testing

AI-assisted: yes
Built and tested in: Codex App
Model: GPT-5.4
Reasoning effort: Extra High
Testing: focused local test

- `pnpm --dir ui exec vitest run --config vitest.node.config.ts src/ui/views/usage-metrics.node.test.ts`

target.title: Keep gateway CLI timeout client-side after accepted runs
target.body:
## Executive Summary

This PR fixes the accepted-timeout regression on the live operational CLI path by keeping `openclaw agent --timeout ...` as a client-side wait budget instead of forwarding it into the gateway-owned accepted run.

## What Was Changed
- Removed forwarded inner timeout from the gateway CLI request in `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts`
- Added focused regression coverage in `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts` asserting the timeout remains client-side and is not forwarded into the accepted run

## What Was Tested
- Targeted test pass for the command path
- Real operational proof via `/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw agent --session-id ... --timeout 1 --json`
- Follow-up completion proof via `agent.wait`

## What Was Verified
- CLI returned `status: accepted` immediately
- Accepted run later completed `status: ok`
- No timeout-continuation prompt was appended to the transcript
- No fallback-model retry / embedded fallback evidence in the proof transcripts
- No session file locked, EPERM, or SQLITE_BUSY tied to the proof sessions

## What Was Not Verified
- Exhaustive behavior across every caller path
- Broader gateway/agent integration beyond the focused test and live operational proofs

## Notes

This PR intentionally excludes unrelated dirty local regression work already present in the repo.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current Copilot Claude policy uses latest-turn-preserving sanitization, allowing persisted thinking into follow-up requests. This review did not run an authenticated live Copilot session.

target.title: [codex] fix gateway hot-mode restart reloads
target.body:
## Summary

Closes the silent-staleness hole in `gateway.reload.mode: "hot"` without changing the shipped default contract. Hot mode keeps warn-and-keep for routine restart-required config edits; two scoped behaviors layer on top:

- **`gateway.reload.autoRestartOnRequired: true`** (new optional key, default `false`): opt-in that delegates restart-required edits to the gateway's existing idle-aware restart path, matching `mode: "hybrid"` for the restart-required branch while keeping hot-safe edits hot.
- **Security-critical carve-out** (always on): restart reasons under `gateway.auth.*` and `secrets.*` schedule a restart even in default hot mode. Without this, a `gateway.auth.token` rotation or `secrets.providers.*` change on disk sits behind warn-and-keep while the runtime keeps honoring the compromised credential.

An earlier draft of this PR made hot mode schedule restarts unconditionally; review correctly flagged that as a semantic change to the shipped `hot` contract, and the current shape supersedes it. Existing `mode: "hot"` deployments are behavior-stable on upgrade for routine edits.

## Details

- `src/gateway/config-reload.ts`: restart-required plans in hot mode now branch three ways — warn-and-keep (default), `hot mode scheduling restart per gateway.reload.autoRestartOnRequired=true` (opt-in), and `hot mode scheduling restart for security-critical change (...)` (always). The existing idle-aware restart machinery (`queueRestart`) remains the only restart path; this PR adds no new restart mechanism.
- `SECURITY_CRITICAL_RESTART_PREFIXES = ["gateway.auth", "secrets"]` — the `secrets` prefix is top-level, fixing the review-found P1 where an earlier `gateway.secrets` prefix could never match real restart reasons; `src/gateway/config-reload-plan.ts` now marks `secrets.providers.*` restart-required so the carve-out has a live trigger.
- `gateway.reload.autoRestartOnRequired` added to `src/config/types.gateway.ts`, `zod-schema.ts` (strict validation), `schema.help.ts`, `schema.labels.ts`.
- Regression tests in `src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts` pin each branch: warn-and-keep preserved on non-security edits; opt-in queues a supervised restart; security-critical auth/secrets changes restart regardless of the opt-in; plus the doctor-style model-route repair still hot-reloads when no restart-only path is present.
- Docs updated: `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, `docs/gateway/configuration.md`, `docs/gateway/index.md` describe the default, the opt-in, and the carve-out.

**Open maintainer decision:** this adds one config key on a compatibility-sensitive surface. The default split (warn-and-keep stays default; auth/secrets always restart) is the proposed contract — if a different split is preferred (e.g. opt-in defaulting `true` for fresh installs only), say the word and I will adjust.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: in hot reload mode, a config write touching any restart-required key was dropped with a single `log.warn` while the gateway kept serving from the stale in-memory snapshot. Observed in production as a 4-hour silent outage where the agent kept resolving the legacy `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` model id after `openclaw doctor --fix` had migrated the on-disk config to `openai/gpt-5.5`; group messages silently dropped, admin DMs returned a canned `[agent failed]` reply. With this patch, security-critical edits (auth/secrets) self-heal via restart by default, and operators can opt all restart-required edits into the same path.

- **Real environment tested**: isolated sandbox gateway on macOS (loopback port 28789), dist built from this branch; plus the original production incident capture on a launchd-supervised deployed gateway (`gateway-host`) documenting the pre-fix failure mode. Sandbox scenarios were captured at commit `d023afb9c4` (the commit introducing the opt-in + carve-out design); the secrets-prefix P1 fix and rebase landed after and are pinned by regression tests.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: for each scenario, start a fresh sandbox gateway from the branch dist, wait for the config watcher to settle, write one config edit, observe the reload log and process state.
  ```
  Scenario A: mode "hot" (defaults), edit gateway.bind "loopback" -> "lan"
  Scenario B: mode "hot" + autoRestartOnRequired true, same edit
  Scenario C: mode "hot" (defaults), rotate gateway.auth.token
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  A: [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (gateway.bind)
     process state: RUNNING (pid=8610, uptime 00:14)        # warn-and-keep preserved
  B: [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode scheduling restart per gateway.reload.autoRestartOnRequired=true (gateway.bind)
     [gateway] received SIGUSR1; restarting
     [shutdown] started: gateway restarting
     process state: RUNNING (pid=6706, uptime 00:15)        # restarted clean
  C: [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode scheduling restart for security-critical change (gateway.auth.token)
     [gateway] received SIGUSR1; restarting
     process state: RUNNING (pid=9261, uptime 00:14)        # credential rotation propagated
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: scenario A proves existing hot-mode deployments keep the shipped warn-and-keep contract with no restart. Scenario B proves the opt-in routes routine restart-required edits through the existing SIGUSR1 + clean-shutdown restart path. Scenario C proves auth rotations propagate by default, closing the credential-rotation-bypass gap. The production failure mode (`hot mode ignoring` on `auth.profiles.*` for 4 hours) is eliminated for security-critical keys and operator-electable for everything else.

- **What was not tested**: launchd auto-respawn end-to-end on hosts using `KeepAlive: { SuccessfulExit: false }` (see operator note below — the clean-exit hand-off needs a kickstart watcher on such hosts; not a regression, the pre-fix path also required manual intervention). The sandbox ran unmanaged (in-process restart mode); the managed-install launchd restart path is covered by existing integration tests. Scenario captures predate the final rebase; the post-rebase head is pinned by 360 passing gateway-config tests including the three branch-behavior regressions.

## Operator note: launchd `SuccessfulExit=false` hosts

Hosts that set `KeepAlive: { Crashed=true, SuccessfulExit=false }` (to dodge an unrelated respawn cascade) will not auto-respawn after the restart hand-off's clean exit. The deployment fork pairs this with a 30s interval watchdog (`launchctl print` state AND port-binding check, then `launchctl kickstart -k`) — documented here so the gap flagged in review is paired with its operational answer. Hosts on default launchd `KeepAlive` semantics respawn normally and need nothing.

target.title: cron: allow retries for local model preflight
target.body:
## Summary
- add configurable retries for isolated cron local-model provider preflight
- expose `cron.modelPreflight.timeoutMs`, `maxAttempts`, and `retryDelayMs`
- document the settings for sleeping/cold-starting Ollama, vLLM, SGLang, LM Studio, and other local providers
- keep defaults unchanged: one 2500ms probe and no retry delay
- enforce one shared 55s preflight deadline across the full fallback candidate chain

## Why
Isolated cron jobs run a lightweight preflight before starting an agent turn for local/private model providers. Current upstream can advance from an unreachable local primary to configured fallback model candidates, but each local candidate still gets only one default 2.5s preflight probe.

That still leaves the cold-start/sleeping-provider case: a local endpoint can become healthy moments after the single probe fails. This PR keeps the fallback behavior intact while letting operators configure a short wake-up window before cron advances to a fallback or marks the run skipped.

The configured per-endpoint retry window is validated at or below 55s, and the runtime now shares one 55s deadline across the complete preflight candidate chain so multi-local fallback paths stay below cron's isolated-agent setup watchdog.

## Review fix
After ClawSweeper noted that the original 55s cap applied per endpoint, this branch now:

- computes one preflight deadline before walking candidates
- passes that same deadline to every candidate preflight
- clamps each local probe timeout and retry delay to the remaining shared budget
- skips additional local endpoint probes once the shared budget is exhausted
- still allows remote/cloud fallback candidates to be selected immediately

## Supplemental verification
- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-preflight.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-fallback-policy.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts` - 113 tests passed across focused config and cron shards
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts` - 55 tests passed, covering the previously failing unrelated CI shard locally
- `pnpm lint --threads=8` - passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/cli/cron.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md docs/providers/ollama.md src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/types.cron.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-preflight.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check` - passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core` - passed

`pnpm check` currently passes all guards except the local `npm-shrinkwrap.json is stale` guard. The ready-for-review CI run on this branch previously passed the shrinkwrap check, so I did not include unrelated generated dependency metadata in this cron patch.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior addressed:** A sleeping or cold-starting local model provider can miss cron's default single 2.5s preflight probe even though it becomes reachable moments later. Configured retries should let that same local candidate wake before cron advances to a fallback or skips the run.

**Real environment tested:** macOS local OpenClaw source checkout rebased onto upstream `main`, Node v22.22.0, pnpm v11.2.2. A real loopback OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint was started after an 800ms delay. The actual `preflightCronModelProvider` runtime used its real network fetch and SSRF-guard path against `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/v1/models`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `pnpm exec tsx -e` live harness that started the local HTTP endpoint after 800ms and invoked `preflightCronModelProvider` twice: first with default settings, then with `{ timeoutMs: 500, maxAttempts: 2, retryDelayMs: 1000 }`.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live console output from the real delayed endpoint run:

```json
{"label":"default-single-attempt","result":{"status":"unavailable","provider":"delayed","model":"delayed-local-model","baseUrl":"http://127.0.0.1:54323/v1","retryAfterMs":300000,"reason":"Agent cron job uses delayed/delayed-local-model but the local provider endpoint is not reachable at http://127.0.0.1:54323/v1. Skipping this cron run after 1 preflight attempt; OpenClaw will retry the provider preflight on a later scheduled run. Last error: TypeError: fetch failed"},"elapsedMs":159,"serverHits":0}
{"label":"configured-retry-window","result":{"status":"available"},"elapsedMs":1019,"serverHits":1}
```

**Observed result after fix:** With defaults, the endpoint was reported unavailable before it woke, confirming the cold-start issue still exists. With the configured retry window, the first connection failed while the endpoint was offline, cron waited, the endpoint started, and the next probe succeeded with `status: "available"` and one observed `/models` request.

**What was not tested:** A separate real Ollama daemon was not stopped and restarted during this run. The proof used a real delayed local OpenAI-compatible endpoint to exercise the same production preflight runtime and network path deterministically.

target.title: Fix session transcript path canonicalization on save
target.body:
# OpenClaw session-store path fix, 2026-04-12

## Status
Applied live to installed runtime. Also ported carefully into a fresh source clone of the matching `v2026.4.11` upstream tag, but not yet merged upstream or installed from that source tree.

## Source clone
- Repo: `/Users/rocklobster/.openclaw/workspace/external/openclaw-upstream`
- Branch: `fix/session-store-transcript-path-canonicalization`

## Protected file
`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/store-Cw5RrVCw.js`

## Backup
`/Users/rocklobster/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/backups/runtime-patches/store-Cw5RrVCw.js.pre-20260412-1233`

## What changed
Added a save-time canonicalization step so persisted `sessionFile` and `transcriptPath` values are rewritten into the real sessions directory before session-store writes hit disk.

## Why
Without this, restored/recovered session entries could keep workspace-style transcript paths, then later be treated as missing/orphaned and get swept or misread.

## Verification performed
- syntax check passed
- synthetic save test passed
- gateway restarted cleanly
- post-restart audit:
  - `entries 913`
  - `legacy_non_agent_keys 0`
  - `non_cron_duplicate_sessionids 0`
  - `missing_jsonl_paths 0`

## Release blocker rule
Treat this as a release blocker until the fix is confirmed upstream and in the installed version. Before any OpenClaw update or manual overwrite touching runtime/config/session storage, stop and verify this protection first.

## Source patch details
Changed files in the source clone:
- `src/config/sessions/store.ts`
- `src/config/sessions/types.ts`
- `src/config/sessions/store.pruning.integration.test.ts`

Validation in source clone:
- `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/config/sessions/store.pruning.integration.test.ts`
- Result: `2 passed`, `29 passed`

## Patch export
- Mailbox-style patch export written for handoff/cherry-pick use:
  - `/Users/rocklobster/.openclaw/workspace/patches/openclaw-session-store-path-fix-2026-04-12.patch`

## Current git state
- Branch is clean and still anchored on tag base `v2026.4.11`
- Head commit:
  - `e1c0d1a` `Fix session transcript path canonicalization on save`

## Next upstream action
Open or stage an upstream PR from the source clone using commit `e1c0d1a` or the exported patch above. Keep the live runtime bandage in place until an official release is installed and re-verified on this machine.

target.title: fix(gemini): bridge OAuth profiles into CLI runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- opt `google-gemini-cli` into explicit OpenClaw auth-profile forwarding
- refresh/load the selected OAuth profile before CLI backend preparation
- materialize selected Gemini OAuth credentials into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json` for the `gemini` subprocess
- keep Gemini CLI auth epochs profile-owned when the bridge is active

Fixes #88742.

## Proof

HEAD: `4ba89fe8b18f7eef6802752c80bfe8f1359f2b2e`

Validation log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-validation.log`

Commands:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts
pnpm -s exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Relevant output:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  59 passed (59)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Source-level behavior proof: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-proof.log`

- `extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts` proves selected OAuth credentials produce an isolated Gemini CLI home and writes `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` without logging token material.
- `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` proves canonical `google/*` models routed through `google-gemini-cli` select the `google-gemini-cli:*` auth profile before `runCliAgent`.

Not tested:

- Full `pnpm -s exec tsgo --noEmit` is blocked on current HEAD by pre-existing errors outside this patch:

```text
src/config/io.ts(192,7): error TS2527: The inferred type of 'configWritePostCommitRollback' references an inaccessible 'unique symbol' type. A type annotation is necessary.
src/secrets/config-io.ts(8,17): error TS4058: Return type of exported function has or is using name 'configWritePostCommitRollback' from external module ".../src/config/io" but cannot be named.
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `google-gemini-cli` backend preparation receives a selected OpenClaw `google-gemini-cli:*` OAuth profile, materializes that OAuth profile into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json`, and passes that isolated home to the real `gemini` subprocess instead of relying on ambient `~/.gemini` state.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Linux workstation, PR branch `fix/88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge`, head `ada77084fb0bce5ccb88ba88b38ed803b5b9f0c6`, OpenClaw `2026.5.31 (7150977)`, `@google/gemini-cli` binary available as `/usr/local/bin/gemini`, real Gemini CLI version `0.40.1`. Token values and account identifiers were not printed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
cd /home/rev/projects/openclaw-prs/pr-88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge
pnpm install
pnpm build
confirmed backend registration path:
  rg -n 'buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend|prepareExecution|CliBackendPlugin|cliBackends' src packages extensions/google | head -120
  nl -ba extensions/google/cli-backend.ts | sed -n '1,155p'
created direct backend proof harness:
  .tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts
ran:
  BAD_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy PROFILE='google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com' pnpm exec tsx ./.tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts 2>&1 | tee /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log
sanitized:
  sed -E -e 's/ya29\.[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-access-token>/g' -e 's#1//[A-Za-z0-9._-]+#<redacted-refresh-token>#g' -e 's/eyJ[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-jwt>/g' -e 's/AIza[[:alnum:]_-]+/<redacted-google-api-key>/g' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log > /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
verified no obvious token material remained:
  grep -E 'ya29\.|1//|eyJ|AIza' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
=== PR 88748 direct Gemini CLI backend proof ===
backend_id=google-gemini-cli
profile=google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com
bad_parent_GEMINI_CLI_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy
prepared_GEMINI_CLI_HOME=/tmp/openclaw/gemini-cli-home-m4iizB
prepared_home_is_bad_parent=false
home_mode=700
gemini_dir_mode=700
oauth_creds_path=/tmp/openclaw/gemini-cli-home-m4iizB/.gemini/oauth_creds.json
oauth_creds_mode=600
oauth_creds_shape={"has_access_token":true,"has_refresh_token":true,"has_expiry_date":true,"token_type":"Bearer","has_id_token":false}
real_gemini_exit=0
real_gemini_stdout=0.40.1
real_gemini_stderr=
cleanup_removed_home=true
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend().prepareExecution(...)` accepted a `google-gemini-cli` OAuth profile, created a private isolated Gemini CLI home, wrote `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` with Bearer OAuth credential shape and restrictive file permissions, returned a `GEMINI_CLI_HOME` different from the deliberately bad parent home, and a real `gemini` subprocess started successfully with that prepared environment. Cleanup removed the isolated home afterward.
- **What was not tested**: The proof intentionally did not print raw Google OAuth access tokens, refresh tokens, ID tokens, or private account data. The subprocess proof used `gemini --version` to prove the real Gemini CLI binary starts under the prepared `GEMINI_CLI_HOME`.

## ClawSweeper follow-up

- Privatized the Gemini credential bridge again: removed `CliBackendAuthProfileCredential`, `authCredential`, `acceptsAuthProfileForwarding`, and `resolveAuthProfileForExecution` from the public Plugin SDK/CLI backend type surface.
- Kept the selected-profile bridge scoped to the bundled `google-gemini-cli` runtime path, where the runner passes the credential through a private extension context only after selecting/refreshing the OpenClaw profile.
- Verified no public references remain:

```text
rg -n "acceptsAuthProfileForwarding|resolveAuthProfileForExecution|CliBackendAuthProfileCredential" src extensions/google docs
# no matches
rg -n "authCredential" src/plugin-sdk src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts
# no matches
```

### Authenticated Gemini prompt proof after bridge privacy fix

Redacted log: `/tmp/pr88748-authenticated-gemini-prompt.redacted.log`

Command shape:

```text
BAD_HOME=$(mktemp -d) pnpm -s exec tsx --eval '<source-level backend proof harness>' 2>&1 \
  | sed -E -e 's/ya29\.[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-access-token>/g' \
           -e 's#1//[A-Za-z0-9._-]+#<redacted-refresh-token>#g' \
           -e 's/eyJ[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-jwt>/g' \
           -e 's/AIza[[:alnum:]_-]+/<redacted-google-api-key>/g' \
  | tee /tmp/pr88748-authenticated-gemini-prompt.redacted.log
```
